--
Adds a new provider for [DeepSeek TUI](https://github.com/Hmbown/DeepSeek-TUI), a terminal-based AI coding as
sistant powered by DeepSeek V4 models.

## Changes

- New package: `pkg/providers/deepseek/` with 5 files (~500 lines)
- Registry: 1-line import + 2-line registration in `pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

## Provider details

**Session format**: JSON files in `~/.deepseek/sessions/<uuid>.json` with metadata (id, model, workspace, tim
estamps) and a messages array supporting text, thinking, tool_use, and tool_result content types.

**Implementation**:
- `Check()` — detects deepseek binary via `exec.LookPath` + `--version`
- `DetectAgent()` — matches sessions by workspace field in metadata
- `GetAgentChatSession/GetAgentChatSessions()` — JSON→schema.SessionData conversion with tool calls, thinking
 content, exchange grouping
- `ExecAgentAndWatch/WatchAgent()` — fsnotify watcher on `~/.deepseek/sessions/`
- `ListAgentChatSessions()` — lightweight metadata without full parsing
- System-injected `<turn_meta>` blocks are filtered from user messages

## Testing

Tested locally:
- `specstory check deepseek` — correctly detects binary and version
- `specstory sync deepseek` — converts sessions to markdown with thinking content in `<details>` blocks, tool
 calls rendered as `<tool-use>` blocks

47	@@ -0,0 +1,536 @@
48	+package deepseek
49	+
50	+import (
51	+	"encoding/json"
52	+	"fmt"
53	+	"log/slog"
54	+	"os"
55	+	"path/filepath"
56	+	"strings"
57	+
58	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
59	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
60	+)
61	+
62	+// Type aliases for convenience - use the shared schema types.
63	+type (
64	+	SessionData  = schema.SessionData
65	+	ProviderInfo = schema.ProviderInfo
66	+	Exchange     = schema.Exchange
67	+	Message      = schema.Message
68	+	ContentPart  = schema.ContentPart
69	+	ToolInfo     = schema.ToolInfo
70	+	Usage        = schema.Usage
71	+)
72	+
73	+// dsSession represents the on-disk format of a DeepSeek TUI session file.
74	+type dsSession struct {
75	+	SchemaVersion int         `json:"schema_version"`
76	+	Metadata      dsMetadata  `json:"metadata"`
77	+	Messages      []dsMessage `json:"messages"`
78	+	SystemPrompt  string      `json:"system_prompt"`
79	+	RawData       string      `json:"-"` // populated after parse for debugRaw output
80	+}
81	+
82	+type dsMetadata struct {
83	+	ID           string `json:"id"`
84	+	Title        string `json:"title"`
85	+	CreatedAt    string `json:"created_at"`
86	+	UpdatedAt    string `json:"updated_at"`
87	+	MessageCount int    `json:"message_count"`
88	+	TotalTokens  int    `json:"total_tokens"`
89	+	Model        string `json:"model"`
90	+	Workspace    string `json:"workspace"`
91	+	Mode         string `json:"mode"`
92	+}
93	+
94	+type dsMessage struct {
95	+	Role    string          `json:"role"`
96	+	Content []dsContentPart `json:"content"`
97	+}
98	+
99	+type dsContentPart struct {
100	+	Type     string `json:"type"`     // "text", "thinking", "tool_use", "tool_result"
101	+	Text     string `json:"text"`     // used when Type == "text"
102	+	Thinking string `json:"thinking"` // used when Type == "thinking"
103	+	// tool_use fields are flat in the JSON (not nested)
104	+	Name  string                 `json:"name"`
105	+	ID    string                 `json:"id"`
106	+	Input map[string]interface{} `json:"input"`
107	+	// tool_result fields
108	+	ToolResultContent string `json:"content"`     // tool_result content (raw string)
109	+	ToolUseID         string `json:"tool_use_id"` // matches the tool_use.id this result is for
110	+}
111	+
112	+// parseSessionFile reads and parses a DeepSeek TUI session JSON file.
113	+func parseSessionFile(path string) (*dsSession, error) {
114	+	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
115	+	if err != nil {
116	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot read session file %s: %w", path, err)
117	+	}
118	+
119	+	var session dsSession
120	+	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &session); err != nil {
121	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot parse session file %s: %w", path, err)
122	+	}
123	+
124	+	session.RawData = string(data)
125	+	return &session, nil
126	+}
127	+
128	+// extractSessionMetadata reads only the metadata fields from a session file
129	+// without parsing the full messages array.
130	+func extractSessionMetadata(path string) (*spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
131	+	// Parse full file since we need to find the first user message for name/slug.
132	+	session, err := parseSessionFile(path)
133	+	if err != nil {
134	+		return nil, err
135	+	}
136	+
137	+	if len(session.Messages) == 0 {
138	+		return nil, nil
139	+	}
140	+
141	+	// Find first user message for slug and name
142	+	var firstUserMsg string
143	+	for _, msg := range session.Messages {
144	+		if msg.Role == "user" {
145	+			firstUserMsg = msgContentText(msg)
146	+			if firstUserMsg != "" {
147	+				break
148	+			}
149	+		}
150	+	}
151	+
152	+	if firstUserMsg == "" {
153	+		return nil, nil
154	+	}
155	+
156	+	slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(firstUserMsg)
157	+	if slug == "" {
158	+		slug = "deepseek-session"
159	+	}
160	+
161	+	name := spi.GenerateReadableName(firstUserMsg)
162	+
163	+	return &spi.SessionMetadata{
164	+		SessionID: session.Metadata.ID,
165	+		CreatedAt: session.Metadata.CreatedAt,
166	+		Slug:      slug,
167	+		Name:      name,
168	+	}, nil
169	+}
170	+
171	+// convertToAgentSession converts a parsed dsSession to the unified AgentChatSession format.
172	+func convertToAgentSession(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
173	+	if session == nil {
174	+		return nil
175	+	}
176	+	if len(session.Messages) == 0 {
177	+		return nil
178	+	}
179	+
180	+	sessionData, err := generateAgentSession(session, workspaceRoot)
181	+	if err != nil {
182	+		slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session due to conversion error",
183	+			"sessionId", session.Metadata.ID, "error", err)
184	+		return nil
185	+	}
186	+
187	+	// Derive slug from first user message
188	+	slug := deriveSlug(session)
189	+	if slug == "" {
190	+		slug = "deepseek-session"
191	+	}
192	+
193	+	if debugRaw {
194	+		writeDebugRaw(session)
195	+	}
196	+
197	+	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
198	+		SessionID:   session.Metadata.ID,
199	+		CreatedAt:   sessionData.CreatedAt,
200	+		Slug:        slug,
201	+		SessionData: sessionData,
202	+		RawData:     session.RawData,
203	+	}
204	+}
205	+
206	+// generateAgentSession converts a dsSession into the shared SessionData schema.
207	+func generateAgentSession(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string) (*SessionData, error) {
208	+	workspace := strings.TrimSpace(workspaceRoot)
209	+	if ws := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.Workspace); ws != "" {
210	+		workspace = ws
211	+	}
212	+
213	+	exchanges := buildExchanges(session, workspace)
214	+	if len(exchanges) == 0 {
215	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("session contains no conversational exchanges")
216	+	}
217	+
218	+	created := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.CreatedAt)
219	+	updated := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.UpdatedAt)
220	+	if updated == "" {
221	+		updated = created
222	+	}
223	+
224	+	// DeepSeek TUI reports only a session-level total_tokens with no breakdown
225	+	// between input/output/cached, so we intentionally don't synthesize a Usage
226	+	// value — attributing the sum to InputTokens would mislead downstream stats.
227	+
228	+	data := &SessionData{
229	+		SchemaVersion: "1.0",
230	+		Provider: ProviderInfo{
231	+			ID:      "deepseek-tui",
232	+			Name:    "DeepSeek TUI",
233	+			Version: session.Metadata.Model,
234	+		},
235	+		SessionID:     session.Metadata.ID,
236	+		CreatedAt:     created,
237	+		UpdatedAt:     updated,
238	+		Slug:          deriveSlug(session),
239	+		WorkspaceRoot: workspace,
240	+		Exchanges:     exchanges,
241	+	}
242	+
243	+	return data, nil
244	+}
245	+
246	+// buildExchanges groups messages into exchanges.
247	+// Each user message with real text content starts a new exchange.
248	+// User messages that only contain tool_result content are treated as
249	+// part of the current assistant turn (tool response), not as new exchanges.
250	+func buildExchanges(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string) []Exchange {
251	+	var exchanges []Exchange
252	+	var current *Exchange
253	+
254	+	// DeepSeek TUI does not stamp per-message timestamps. Use session-level
255	+	// CreatedAt for user turns (best signal of when the conversation began)
256	+	// and UpdatedAt for assistant turns so re-parsing the same file produces
257	+	// stable, deterministic output. time.Now() would change every parse.
258	+	userTs := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.CreatedAt)
259	+	assistantTs := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.UpdatedAt)
260	+	if assistantTs == "" {
261	+		assistantTs = userTs
262	+	}
263	+
264	+	for _, msg := range session.Messages {
265	+		if msg.Role == "user" {
266	+			if isToolResultOnly(&msg) {
267	+				// Tool results live in user-role messages; route each tool_result
268	+				// to the assistant Tool message it belongs to via tool_use_id.
269	+				if current != nil {
270	+					attachToolResults(&msg, current)
271	+				}
272	+				continue
273	+			}
274	+
275	+			if current != nil && len(current.Messages) > 0 {
276	+				exchanges = append(exchanges, *current)
277	+			}
278	+			current = &Exchange{}
279	+
280	+			spiMsg := convertUserMessage(msg, userTs)
281	+			if len(spiMsg.Content) > 0 {
282	+				current.Messages = append(current.Messages, spiMsg)
283	+				if current.StartTime == "" {
284	+					current.StartTime = userTs
285	+				}
286	+				current.EndTime = userTs
287	+			}
288	+			continue
289	+		}
290	+
291	+		if current == nil {
292	+			current = &Exchange{}
293	+		}
294	+
295	+		spiMsgs := convertAssistantMessage(msg, session.Metadata.Model, assistantTs, workspaceRoot)
296	+		current.Messages = append(current.Messages, spiMsgs...)
297	+		current.EndTime = assistantTs
298	+	}
299	+
300	+	if current != nil && len(current.Messages) > 0 {
301	+		exchanges = append(exchanges, *current)
302	+	}
303	+
304	+	for i := range exchanges {
305	+		exchanges[i].ExchangeID = fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", session.Metadata.ID, i)
306	+		if exchanges[i].StartTime == "" {
307	+			exchanges[i].StartTime = session.Metadata.CreatedAt
308	+		}
309	+		if exchanges[i].EndTime == "" {
310	+			exchanges[i].EndTime = exchanges[i].StartTime
311	+		}
312	+	}
313	+
314	+	return exchanges
315	+}
316	+
317	+// isToolResultOnly returns true if the user message only contains tool_result
318	+// content parts (no real text from the user).
319	+func isToolResultOnly(msg *dsMessage) bool {
320	+	hasToolResult := false
321	+	for _, cp := range msg.Content {
322	+		switch cp.Type {
323	+		case "tool_result":
324	+			hasToolResult = true
325	+		case "text":
326	+			// Check if the text content is just <turn_meta> or system noise.
327	+			t := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
328	+			if t != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(t, "<turn_meta>") {
329	+				return false // has real user text
330	+			}
331	+		}
332	+	}
333	+	return hasToolResult
334	+}
335	+
336	+// attachToolResults routes each tool_result content part in a user-role message
337	+// to the assistant Tool message it belongs to, matched by tool_use_id.
338	+//
339	+// DeepSeek TUI emits tool results in the user message that follows the
340	+// assistant's tool calls. When the assistant fires N parallel tool calls, the
341	+// next user message holds N tool_result parts — each with its own tool_use_id.
342	+// Earlier code attached every result to whichever was the *last* tool message
343	+// and string-concatenated multiple results together; that produced wrong
344	+// markdown for any session with parallel calls. We fix this by indexing
345	+// assistant tool messages by UseID and attaching each result to its match.
346	+//
347	+// After attaching, we re-render FormattedMarkdown so the rendered tool block
348	+// includes the result body.
349	+func attachToolResults(msg *dsMessage, current *Exchange) {
350	+	if current == nil {
351	+		return
352	+	}
353	+	// Build an index of assistant Tool messages in this exchange by UseID.
354	+	byUseID := make(map[string]*Message, len(current.Messages))
355	+	for i := range current.Messages {
356	+		m := &current.Messages[i]
357	+		if m.Tool != nil && m.Tool.UseID != "" {
358	+			byUseID[m.Tool.UseID] = m
359	+		}
360	+	}
361	+
362	+	for _, cp := range msg.Content {
363	+		if cp.Type != "tool_result" {
364	+			continue
365	+		}
366	+		target, ok := byUseID[cp.ToolUseID]
367	+		if !ok || target == nil || target.Tool == nil {
368	+			// Result with no matching assistant call — drop rather than misattribute.
369	+			slog.Debug("deepseek: tool_result has no matching tool_use",
370	+				"toolUseId", cp.ToolUseID)
371	+			continue
372	+		}
373	+		content := strings.TrimSpace(cp.ToolResultContent)
374	+		if content == "" {
375	+			continue
376	+		}
377	+		target.Tool.Output = map[string]interface{}{"content": content}
378	+		// Re-render the formatted markdown now that the result is in place.
379	+		formatted := formatToolCall(target.Tool)
380	+		if formatted != "" {
381	+			target.Tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formatted
382	+		}
383	+	}
384	+}
385	+
386	+// convertUserMessage converts a DeepSeek user message to the schema Message format.
387	+// System-injected metadata blocks like <turn_meta> are filtered out so only the
388	+// user's actual question appears in the saved session.
389	+func convertUserMessage(msg dsMessage, timestamp string) Message {
390	+	parts := userContentParts(msg)
391	+	return Message{
392	+		Timestamp: timestamp,
393	+		Role:      "user",
394	+		Content:   parts,
395	+	}
396	+}
397	+
398	+// userContentParts returns the user-visible text content parts from a message,
399	+// skipping system-injected blocks such as <turn_meta>.
400	+func userContentParts(msg dsMessage) []ContentPart {
401	+	var parts []ContentPart
402	+	for _, cp := range msg.Content {
403	+		if cp.Type != "text" || cp.Text == "" {
404	+			continue
405	+		}
406	+		if strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text), "<turn_meta>") {
407	+			continue
408	+		}
409	+		parts = append(parts, ContentPart{Type: "text", Text: cp.Text})
410	+	}
411	+	return parts
412	+}
413	+
414	+// convertAssistantMessage converts a DeepSeek assistant message to the schema Message format.
415	+// Each tool_use content part produces a separate Message (one tool = one message),
416	+// while text and thinking parts are combined into a single message.
417	+func convertAssistantMessage(msg dsMessage, model string, timestamp string, workspaceRoot string) []Message {
418	+	var msgs []Message
419	+	var textParts []ContentPart
420	+	var toolParts []dsContentPart
421	+
422	+	for _, cp := range msg.Content {
423	+		switch cp.Type {
424	+		case "tool_use":
425	+			toolParts = append(toolParts, cp)
426	+		case "thinking":
427	+			thinking := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Thinking)
428	+			if thinking == "" {
429	+				thinking = strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
430	+			}
431	+			if thinking != "" {
432	+				textParts = append(textParts, ContentPart{Type: "thinking", Text: thinking})
433	+			}
434	+		case "text":
435	+			text := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
436	+			if text == "" {
437	+				text = strings.TrimSpace(cp.Thinking)
438	+			}
439	+			if text != "" {
440	+				textParts = append(textParts, ContentPart{Type: "text", Text: text})
441	+			}
442	+		}
443	+	}
444	+
445	+	if len(textParts) > 0 {
446	+		msgs = append(msgs, Message{
447	+			Timestamp: timestamp,
448	+			Role:      "agent",
449	+			Model:     model,
450	+			Content:   textParts,
451	+		})
452	+	}
453	+
454	+	for _, cp := range toolParts {
455	+		msgs = append(msgs, convertToolUseMessage(cp, model, timestamp, workspaceRoot))
456	+	}
457	+
458	+	return msgs
459	+}
460	+
461	+// convertToolUseMessage creates a Message for a tool_use content part. The
462	+// FormattedMarkdown is set from the tool's input only here; attachToolResults
463	+// re-renders it once the matching tool_result is attached.
464	+func convertToolUseMessage(cp dsContentPart, model string, timestamp string, workspaceRoot string) Message {
465	+	name := cp.Name
466	+	if name == "" {
467	+		name = "unknown"
468	+	}
469	+	tool := &ToolInfo{
470	+		Name:  name,
471	+		Type:  classifyToolType(name),
472	+		UseID: cp.ID,
473	+		Input: cp.Input,
474	+	}
475	+	if formatted := formatToolCall(tool); formatted != "" {
476	+		tool.FormattedMarkdown = &formatted
477	+	}
478	+	return Message{
479	+		Timestamp: timestamp,
480	+		Role:      "agent",
481	+		Model:     model,
482	+		Tool:      tool,
483	+		PathHints: extractPathHints(cp.Input, workspaceRoot),
484	+	}
485	+}
486	+
487	+// classifyToolType maps a tool name to a SpecStory tool type.
488	+func classifyToolType(name string) string {
489	+	writeTools := map[string]bool{
490	+		"write_file": true, "edit_file": true, "apply_patch": true,
491	+	}
492	+	readTools := map[string]bool{
493	+		"read_file": true, "list_dir": true, "retrieve_tool_result": true,
494	+	}
495	+	searchTools := map[string]bool{
496	+		"grep_files": true, "file_search": true, "web_search": true,
497	+		"fetch_url": true,
498	+	}
499	+	shellTools := map[string]bool{
500	+		"exec_shell": true, "task_shell_start": true, "task_shell_wait": true,
501	+		"exec_shell_wait": true, "exec_interact": true, "exec_shell_interact": true,
502	+	}
503	+	taskTools := map[string]bool{
504	+		"update_plan": true, "todo_write": true, "checklist_write": true,
505	+		"task_create": true, "task_read": true, "task_list": true,
506	+	}
507	+
508	+	if writeTools[name] {
509	+		return schema.ToolTypeWrite
510	+	}
511	+	if readTools[name] {
512	+		return schema.ToolTypeRead
513	+	}
514	+	if searchTools[name] {
515	+		return schema.ToolTypeSearch
516	+	}
517	+	if shellTools[name] {
518	+		return schema.ToolTypeShell
519	+	}
520	+	if taskTools[name] {
521	+		return schema.ToolTypeTask
522	+	}
523	+
524	+	return schema.ToolTypeGeneric
525	+}
526	+
527	+// msgContentText extracts the first user-visible text content from a dsMessage,
528	+// skipping system-injected blocks such as <turn_meta>.
529	+func msgContentText(msg dsMessage) string {
530	+	for _, cp := range msg.Content {
531	+		if cp.Type == "text" && cp.Text != "" {
532	+			if strings.HasPrefix(strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text), "<turn_meta>") {
533	+				continue
534	+			}
535	+			return cp.Text
536	+		}
537	+	}
538	+	// Fallback: try thinking content or first non-empty.
539	+	for _, cp := range msg.Content {
540	+		t := strings.TrimSpace(cp.Text)
541	+		if t == "" {
542	+			t = strings.TrimSpace(cp.Thinking)
543	+		}
544	+		if t != "" {
545	+			return t
546	+		}
547	+	}
548	+	return ""
549	+}
550	+
551	+// deriveSlug creates a filename-safe slug from the first user message.
552	+func deriveSlug(session *dsSession) string {
553	+	for _, msg := range session.Messages {
554	+		if msg.Role == "user" {
555	+			text := msgContentText(msg)
556	+			if text != "" {
557	+				return spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(text)
558	+			}
559	+		}
560	+	}
561	+	return "deepseek-session"
562	+}
563	+
564	+// writeDebugRaw writes the raw session JSON to the debug directory.
565	+func writeDebugRaw(session *dsSession) {
566	+	if session == nil {
567	+		return
568	+	}
569	+
570	+	dir := spi.GetDebugDir(session.Metadata.ID)
571	+	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
572	+		slog.Debug("deepseek: unable to create debug dir", "error", err)
573	+		return
574	+	}
575	+
576	+	rawPath := filepath.Join(dir, "raw-session.json")
577	+	if err := os.WriteFile(rawPath, []byte(session.RawData), 0o644); err != nil {
578	+		slog.Debug("deepseek: failed to write debug raw file", "path", rawPath, "error", err)
579	+		return
580	+	}
581	+
582	+	slog.Debug("deepseek: wrote debug raw file", "sessionId", session.Metadata.ID, "path", rawPath)
583	+}

600	+// TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps: re-parsing the same session must
601	+// produce identical EndTimes. Fix: buildExchanges uses UpdatedAt (fallback
602	+// CreatedAt) instead of time.Now() so output stays byte-stable.
603	+func TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps(t *testing.T) {
604	+	session := &dsSession{
605	+		Metadata: dsMetadata{
606	+			ID:        "sess-stable",
607	+			CreatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z",
608	+			UpdatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z",
609	+		},
610	+		Messages: []dsMessage{
611	+			{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hi"}}},
612	+			{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hello"}}},
613	+		},
614	+	}
615	+
616	+	first := buildExchanges(session, "")
617	+	second := buildExchanges(session, "")
618	+
619	+	if len(first) != 1 || len(second) != 1 {
620	+		t.Fatalf("expected 1 exchange each, got %d / %d", len(first), len(second))
621	+	}
622	+	if first[0].EndTime != second[0].EndTime {
623	+		t.Errorf("EndTime not deterministic: %q vs %q", first[0].EndTime, second[0].EndTime)
624	+	}
625	+	if first[0].EndTime != "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z" {
626	+		t.Errorf("expected EndTime=UpdatedAt, got %q", first[0].EndTime)
627	+	}
628	+	if first[0].StartTime != "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z" {
629	+		t.Errorf("expected StartTime=CreatedAt, got %q", first[0].StartTime)
630	+	}
631	+}
632	+
633	+// TestBuildExchanges_FallbackToCreatedAt: assistantTs falls back to CreatedAt when UpdatedAt is empty.
634	+func TestBuildExchanges_FallbackToCreatedAt(t *testing.T) {
635	+	session := &dsSession{
636	+		Metadata: dsMetadata{
637	+			ID:        "sess-fallback",

639	+			// UpdatedAt intentionally empty
640	+		},
641	+		Messages: []dsMessage{

643	+			{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hello"}}},
644	+		},
645	+	}
646	+
647	+	exchanges := buildExchanges(session, "")
648	+	if len(exchanges) != 1 {
649	+		t.Fatalf("expected 1 exchange, got %d", len(exchanges))
650	+	}
651	+	if exchanges[0].EndTime != "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z" {
652	+		t.Errorf("expected EndTime to fall back to CreatedAt, got %q", exchanges[0].EndTime)
653	+	}
654	+}

656	+// TestAttachToolResults_ParallelToolCalls: when the assistant emits parallel
657	+// tool calls (id=A, id=B) and the next user message contains tool_results for

883	+				{Type: "tool_use", ID: "x"},
884	+			}},
885	+			wantCount: 1,
886	+			wantChecks: func(t *testing.T, msgs []Message) {
887	+				if msgs[0].Tool == nil || msgs[0].Tool.Name != "unknown" {
888	+					t.Errorf("expected tool name 'unknown', got %v", msgs[0].Tool)
889	+				}
890	+			},
891	+		},
892	+	}
893	+
894	+	for _, tt := range tests {
895	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
896	+			msgs := convertAssistantMessage(tt.msg, "deepseek-r1", "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z", "")
897	+			if len(msgs) != tt.wantCount {
898	+				t.Fatalf("expected %d messages, got %d", tt.wantCount, len(msgs))
899	+			}
900	+			tt.wantChecks(t, msgs)
901	+		})
902	+	}
903	+}

905	+func TestGenerateAgentSession_MetadataWorkspaceAndUsage(t *testing.T) {
906	+	tests := []struct {
907	+		name              string
908	+		metadataWorkspace string
909	+		callerWorkspace   string
910	+		wantWorkspace     string
911	+	}{
912	+		{
913	+			name:              "metadata workspace overrides caller workspace",
914	+			metadataWorkspace: "/metadata/workspace",
915	+			callerWorkspace:   "/caller/workspace",
916	+			wantWorkspace:     "/metadata/workspace",
917	+		},
918	+		{
919	+			name:              "caller workspace used when metadata workspace empty",
920	+			metadataWorkspace: "",
921	+			callerWorkspace:   "/caller/workspace",
922	+			wantWorkspace:     "/caller/workspace",
923	+		},
924	+		{
925	+			name:              "whitespace metadata workspace does not override caller workspace",
926	+			metadataWorkspace: "   ",
927	+			callerWorkspace:   "/caller/workspace",
928	+			wantWorkspace:     "/caller/workspace",
929	+		},
930	+	}
931	+
932	+	for _, tt := range tests {
933	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
934	+			session := &dsSession{
935	+				Metadata: dsMetadata{
936	+					ID:          "sess-metadata",
937	+					CreatedAt:   "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z",
938	+					UpdatedAt:   "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z",
939	+					TotalTokens: 12345,
940	+					Model:       "deepseek-r1",
941	+					Workspace:   tt.metadataWorkspace,
942	+				},
943	+				Messages: []dsMessage{
944	+					{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hello"}}},
945	+					{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hi"}}},
946	+				},
947	+			}
948	+
949	+			data, err := generateAgentSession(session, tt.callerWorkspace)
950	+			if err != nil {
951	+				t.Fatalf("generateAgentSession() error = %v", err)
952	+			}
953	+			if data.Provider.ID != "deepseek-tui" {
954	+				t.Errorf("Provider.ID = %q, want deepseek-tui", data.Provider.ID)
955	+			}
956	+			if data.Provider.Name != "DeepSeek TUI" {
957	+				t.Errorf("Provider.Name = %q, want DeepSeek TUI", data.Provider.Name)
958	+			}
959	+			if data.Provider.Version != "deepseek-r1" {
960	+				t.Errorf("Provider.Version = %q, want deepseek-r1", data.Provider.Version)
961	+			}
962	+			if data.WorkspaceRoot != tt.wantWorkspace {
963	+				t.Errorf("WorkspaceRoot = %q, want %q", data.WorkspaceRoot, tt.wantWorkspace)
964	+			}
965	+
966	+			for _, exchange := range data.Exchanges {
967	+				for _, msg := range exchange.Messages {
968	+					if msg.Usage != nil {
969	+						t.Fatalf("TotalTokens should not synthesize per-message Usage, got %+v", msg.Usage)
970	+					}
971	+				}
972	+			}
973	+		})
974	+	}
975	+}

977	+func TestConvertAssistantMessage_AttachesPathHints(t *testing.T) {
978	+	msgs := convertAssistantMessage(dsMessage{Content: []dsContentPart{
979	+		{Type: "tool_use", Name: "read_file", ID: "read", Input: map[string]interface{}{"file_path": "/repo/pkg/main.go"}},
980	+		{Type: "tool_use", Name: "exec_shell", ID: "shell", Input: map[string]interface{}{"command": "echo hi > out.txt", "workdir": "/repo"}},
981	+	}}, "deepseek-r1", "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z", "/repo")
982	+
983	+	if len(msgs) != 2 {
984	+		t.Fatalf("expected 2 tool messages, got %d", len(msgs))
985	+	}
986	+	if !reflect.DeepEqual(msgs[0].PathHints, []string{"pkg/main.go"}) {
987	+		t.Errorf("read_file PathHints = %v, want [pkg/main.go]", msgs[0].PathHints)
988	+	}
989	+	foundShellOutput := false
990	+	for _, hint := range msgs[1].PathHints {
991	+		if hint == "out.txt" {
992	+			foundShellOutput = true
993	+			break
994	+		}
995	+	}
996	+	if !foundShellOutput {
997	+		t.Errorf("exec_shell PathHints = %v, want to contain out.txt", msgs[1].PathHints)
998	+	}
999	+}

1025	+func TestDeriveSlug(t *testing.T) {
1026	+	tests := []struct {
1027	+		name    string
1028	+		session *dsSession
1029	+		want    string // empty means "non-empty"; specific string means exact match
1030	+	}{
1031	+		{
1032	+			name: "first user message becomes slug source",
1033	+			session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{
1034	+				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "Help me refactor the parser"}}},
1035	+			}},
1036	+		},
1037	+		{
1038	+			name: "skips assistant messages and turn_meta blocks",
1039	+			session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{
1040	+				{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "ignore me"}}},
1041	+				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "<turn_meta>noise"}}},
1042	+				{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "Real question here"}}},
1043	+			}},
1044	+		},
1045	+		{
1046	+			name:    "empty session yields default slug",
1047	+			session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{}},
1048	+			want:    "deepseek-session",
1049	+		},
1050	+		{
1051	+			name: "no user messages yields default slug",
1052	+			session: &dsSession{Messages: []dsMessage{
1053	+				{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "hi"}}},
1054	+			}},
1055	+			want: "deepseek-session",
1056	+		},
1057	+	}
1058	+
1059	+	for _, tt := range tests {
1060	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
1061	+			got := deriveSlug(tt.session)
1062	+			if tt.want != "" {
1063	+				if got != tt.want {
1064	+					t.Errorf("deriveSlug() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
1065	+				}
1066	+				return
1067	+			}
1068	+			if got == "" || got == "deepseek-session" {
1069	+				t.Errorf("expected non-default slug, got %q", got)
1070	+			}
1071	+		})
1072	+	}
1073	+}

1075	+// TestBuildExchanges_TwoTurns covers exchange grouping: a real user turn
1076	+// boundary should start a new exchange while tool-result-only user messages
1077	+// stay attached to the current assistant turn.
1078	+func TestBuildExchanges_TwoTurns(t *testing.T) {
1079	+	session := &dsSession{
1080	+		Metadata: dsMetadata{
1081	+			ID:        "sess-2turn",
1082	+			CreatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z",
1083	+			UpdatedAt: "2026-05-01T10:05:00Z",
1084	+		},
1085	+		Messages: []dsMessage{
1086	+			{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "first question"}}},
1087	+			{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{
1088	+				{Type: "tool_use", Name: "read_file", ID: "A"},
1089	+			}},
1090	+			{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{
1091	+				{Type: "tool_result", ToolUseID: "A", ToolResultContent: "result"},
1092	+			}},
1093	+			{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "answer one"}}},
1094	+			{Role: "user", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "second question"}}},
1095	+			{Role: "assistant", Content: []dsContentPart{{Type: "text", Text: "answer two"}}},
1096	+		},
1097	+	}
1098	+
1099	+	exchanges := buildExchanges(session, "")
1100	+	if len(exchanges) != 2 {
1101	+		t.Fatalf("expected 2 exchanges, got %d", len(exchanges))
1102	+	}
1103	+
1104	+	// Exchange IDs should be deterministic and indexed.
1105	+	if exchanges[0].ExchangeID != "sess-2turn:0" {
1106	+		t.Errorf("expected first ExchangeID 'sess-2turn:0', got %q", exchanges[0].ExchangeID)
1107	+	}
1108	+	if exchanges[1].ExchangeID != "sess-2turn:1" {
1109	+		t.Errorf("expected second ExchangeID 'sess-2turn:1', got %q", exchanges[1].ExchangeID)
1110	+	}
1111	+}
1112	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseek/deepseek_exec.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseek/deepseek_exec.go
1113	new file mode 100644
1114	index 0000000..0eed5f1
1115	--- /dev/null
1116	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseek/deepseek_exec.go
1117	@@ -0,0 +1,82 @@
1118	+package deepseek
1119	+
1120	+import (
1121	+	"log/slog"
1122	+	"os"
1123	+	"os/exec"
1124	+	"path/filepath"
1125	+	"strings"
1126	+
1127	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
1128	+)
1129	+
1130	+// expandTilde expands a leading ~ to the user's home directory so users can
1131	+// configure custom commands like "~/bin/deepseek".
1132	+func expandTilde(path string) string {
1133	+	if !strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {
1134	+		return path
1135	+	}
1136	+	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
1137	+	if err != nil {
1138	+		return path
1139	+	}
1140	+	return filepath.Join(home, path[2:])
1141	+}
1142	+
1143	+// parseCommand splits a custom command string into executable name and args.
1144	+// Returns default command if customCommand is empty.
1145	+func parseCommand(customCommand string) (string, []string) {
1146	+	if strings.TrimSpace(customCommand) != "" {
1147	+		parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
1148	+		if len(parts) > 0 {
1149	+			return expandTilde(parts[0]), parts[1:]
1150	+		}
1151	+	}
1152	+	return defaultCommand, nil
1153	+}
1154	+
1155	+// ExecuteDeepSeek launches the DeepSeek TUI process and blocks until it exits.
1156	+func ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand string, resumeSessionID string) error {
1157	+	cmdName, args := parseCommand(customCommand)
1158	+	args = ensureResumeArgs(args, resumeSessionID)
1159	+	if resumeSessionID != "" {
1160	+		slog.Info("ExecuteDeepSeek: resuming session", "sessionId", resumeSessionID)
1161	+	}
1162	+
1163	+	command := exec.Command(cmdName, args...)
1164	+	command.Stdin = os.Stdin
1165	+	command.Stdout = os.Stdout
1166	+	command.Stderr = os.Stderr
1167	+
1168	+	return command.Run()
1169	+}
1170	+
1171	+// ensureResumeArgs adds --resume <sessionID> to args if not already present.
1172	+// DeepSeek TUI uses --resume to continue an existing session.
1173	+func ensureResumeArgs(args []string, resumeSessionID string) []string {
1174	+	if resumeSessionID == "" {
1175	+		return args
1176	+	}
1177	+
1178	+	for i, arg := range args {
1179	+		if arg == "--resume" || arg == "-r" {
1180	+			if i+1 < len(args) && strings.TrimSpace(args[i+1]) != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(args[i+1], "-") {
1181	+				return args
1182	+			}
1183	+			repaired := append([]string{}, args[:i+1]...)
1184	+			repaired = append(repaired, resumeSessionID)
1185	+			repaired = append(repaired, args[i+1:]...)
1186	+			return repaired
1187	+		}
1188	+		if strings.HasPrefix(arg, "--resume=") {
1189	+			if strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(arg, "--resume=")) != "" {
1190	+				return args
1191	+			}
1192	+			repaired := append([]string{}, args...)
1193	+			repaired[i] = "--resume=" + resumeSessionID
1194	+			return repaired
1195	+		}
1196	+	}
1197	+
1198	+	return append(args, "--resume", resumeSessionID)
1199	+}

1216	+func TestEnsureResumeArgs(t *testing.T) {
1217	+	tests := []struct {
1218	+		name            string
1219	+		args            []string
1220	+		resumeSessionID string
1221	+		expected        []string
1222	+	}{
1223	+		{
1224	+			name:            "returns args unchanged when resumeSessionID is empty",
1225	+			args:            []string{"--verbose"},
1226	+			resumeSessionID: "",
1227	+			expected:        []string{"--verbose"},
1228	+		},
1229	+		{
1230	+			name:            "returns args unchanged when --resume already has value",
1231	+			args:            []string{"--resume", "existing-session"},
1232	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1233	+			expected:        []string{"--resume", "existing-session"},
1234	+		},
1235	+		{
1236	+			name:            "returns args unchanged when -r already has value",
1237	+			args:            []string{"-r", "existing-session"},
1238	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1239	+			expected:        []string{"-r", "existing-session"},
1240	+		},
1241	+		{
1242	+			name:            "returns args unchanged when --resume=value exists",
1243	+			args:            []string{"--resume=existing-session"},
1244	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1245	+			expected:        []string{"--resume=existing-session"},
1246	+		},
1247	+		{
1248	+			name:            "repairs dangling --resume",
1249	+			args:            []string{"--resume"},
1250	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1251	+			expected:        []string{"--resume", "new-session"},
1252	+		},
1253	+		{
1254	+			name:            "repairs dangling -r",
1255	+			args:            []string{"-r"},
1256	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1257	+			expected:        []string{"-r", "new-session"},
1258	+		},
1259	+		{
1260	+			name:            "repairs --resume followed by another flag",
1261	+			args:            []string{"--resume", "--verbose"},
1262	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1263	+			expected:        []string{"--resume", "new-session", "--verbose"},
1264	+		},
1265	+		{
1266	+			name:            "repairs empty --resume equals form",
1267	+			args:            []string{"--resume="},
1268	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1269	+			expected:        []string{"--resume=new-session"},
1270	+		},
1271	+		{
1272	+			name:            "appends --resume when not present",
1273	+			args:            []string{"--verbose"},
1274	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1275	+			expected:        []string{"--verbose", "--resume", "new-session"},
1276	+		},
1277	+		{
1278	+			name:            "appends --resume when args is nil",
1279	+			args:            nil,
1280	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1281	+			expected:        []string{"--resume", "new-session"},
1282	+		},
1283	+		{
1284	+			name:            "appends --resume when args is empty",
1285	+			args:            []string{},
1286	+			resumeSessionID: "new-session",
1287	+			expected:        []string{"--resume", "new-session"},
1288	+		},
1289	+	}
1290	+
1291	+	for _, tt := range tests {
1292	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
1293	+			got := ensureResumeArgs(tt.args, tt.resumeSessionID)
1294	+			if !reflect.DeepEqual(got, tt.expected) {
1295	+				t.Errorf("ensureResumeArgs() = %v, want %v", got, tt.expected)
1296	+			}
1297	+		})
1298	+	}
1299	+}

1301	+func TestParseCommand(t *testing.T) {
1302	+	tests := []struct {
1303	+		name        string
1304	+		input       string
1305	+		wantCmd     string
1306	+		wantArgs    []string
1307	+		wantHomeFix bool
1308	+	}{
1309	+		{
1310	+			name:     "empty input returns default",
1311	+			input:    "",
1312	+			wantCmd:  defaultCommand,
1313	+			wantArgs: nil,
1314	+		},
1315	+		{
1316	+			name:     "whitespace-only input returns default",
1317	+			input:    "   ",
1318	+			wantCmd:  defaultCommand,
1319	+			wantArgs: nil,
1320	+		},
1321	+		{
1322	+			name:     "single token returns command and no args",
1323	+			input:    "/usr/local/bin/deepseek",
1324	+			wantCmd:  "/usr/local/bin/deepseek",
1325	+			wantArgs: []string{},
1326	+		},
1327	+		{
1328	+			name:     "command plus args splits on whitespace",
1329	+			input:    "deepseek --debug --workdir /tmp",
1330	+			wantCmd:  "deepseek",
1331	+			wantArgs: []string{"--debug", "--workdir", "/tmp"},
1332	+		},
1333	+		{
1334	+			name:     "quoted custom command args are preserved",
1335	+			input:    "deepseek --config \"/tmp/path with spaces/config.json\" --prompt 'hello world'",
1336	+			wantCmd:  "deepseek",
1337	+			wantArgs: []string{"--config", "/tmp/path with spaces/config.json", "--prompt", "hello world"},
1338	+		},
1339	+		{
1340	+			name:        "leading tilde is expanded to home",
1341	+			input:       "~/bin/deepseek --verbose",
1342	+			wantArgs:    []string{"--verbose"},
1343	+			wantHomeFix: true,
1344	+		},
1345	+	}
1346	+
1347	+	home, _ := os.UserHomeDir()
1348	+
1349	+	for _, tt := range tests {
1350	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
1351	+			gotCmd, gotArgs := parseCommand(tt.input)
1352	+
1353	+			if tt.wantHomeFix {
1354	+				expected := filepath.Join(home, "bin/deepseek")
1355	+				if gotCmd != expected {
1356	+					t.Errorf("parseCommand() cmd = %q, want %q", gotCmd, expected)
1357	+				}
1358	+			} else if gotCmd != tt.wantCmd {
1359	+				t.Errorf("parseCommand() cmd = %q, want %q", gotCmd, tt.wantCmd)
1360	+			}
1361	+
1362	+			if !reflect.DeepEqual(gotArgs, tt.wantArgs) {
1363	+				t.Errorf("parseCommand() args = %v, want %v", gotArgs, tt.wantArgs)
1364	+			}
1365	+		})
1366	+	}
1367	+}

1369	+func TestExpandTilde(t *testing.T) {
1370	+	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
1371	+	if err != nil {
1372	+		t.Skip("UserHomeDir failed, skipping")
1373	+	}
1374	+
1375	+	tests := []struct {
1376	+		name  string
1377	+		input string
1378	+		want  string
1379	+	}{
1380	+		{name: "no tilde returns input", input: "/usr/local/bin/foo", want: "/usr/local/bin/foo"},
1381	+		{name: "bare tilde without slash returns input", input: "~tilde", want: "~tilde"},
1382	+		{name: "tilde slash expands to home", input: "~/bin/foo", want: filepath.Join(home, "bin/foo")},
1383	+		{name: "empty input returns empty", input: "", want: ""},
1384	+	}
1385	+
1386	+	for _, tt := range tests {
1387	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
1388	+			got := expandTilde(tt.input)
1389	+			if got != tt.want {
1390	+				t.Errorf("expandTilde(%q) = %q, want %q", tt.input, got, tt.want)
1391	+			}
1392	+		})
1393	+	}
1394	+}

1406	+func TestParseCommand_DoesNotMutate(t *testing.T) {
1407	+	// Guard against accidental in-place modification of the input string,
1408	+	// which would surprise callers that re-use the same custom-command value.
1409	+	input := "deepseek --foo"
1410	+	original := input
1411	+	_, _ = parseCommand(input)
1412	+	if input != original {
1413	+		t.Errorf("parseCommand mutated input: got %q, want %q", input, original)
1414	+	}
1415	+	// Also verify args slice is independent (we can mutate without aliasing into spi internals).
1416	+	_, args := parseCommand("deepseek --a --b")
1417	+	if len(args) > 0 {
1418	+		args[0] = "MUTATED"
1419	+		_, args2 := parseCommand("deepseek --a --b")
1420	+		if strings.Contains(strings.Join(args2, " "), "MUTATED") {
1421	+			t.Errorf("parseCommand returned aliased slice; got %v", args2)
1422	+		}
1423	+	}
1424	+}

1431	+package deepseek
1432	+
1433	+import (
1434	+	"encoding/json"
1435	+	"fmt"
1436	+	"path/filepath"

1443	+
1444	+// formatToolCall renders a ToolInfo into a markdown body that the session
1445	+// renderer wraps in a <tool-use><details> block. The body combines a tool's
1446	+// input rendering with its (optional) result. DeepSeek attaches results in a
1447	+// later user message, so this function is called twice: once when the tool_use
1448	+// is converted (input only), then again from attachToolResults once the
1449	+// matching tool_result is in place.
1450	+func formatToolCall(tool *ToolInfo) string {
1451	+	if tool == nil {
1452	+		return ""
1453	+	}
1454	+	body := strings.TrimSpace(formatToolInput(tool))
1455	+	result := strings.TrimSpace(formatToolOutput(tool))
1456	+
1457	+	var parts []string
1458	+	if body != "" {
1459	+		parts = append(parts, body)
1460	+	}
1461	+	if result != "" {
1462	+		parts = append(parts, result)
1463	+	}
1464	+	return strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(parts, "\n\n"))
1465	+}
1466	+
1467	+func formatToolInput(tool *ToolInfo) string {
1468	+	args := tool.Input
1469	+	switch normalizeToolName(tool.Name) {
1470	+	case "execshell", "execshellwait", "execinteract", "execshellinteract",
1471	+		"taskshellstart", "taskshellwait":
1472	+		return formatExecuteInput(args)
1473	+	case "readfile":
1474	+		return renderReadInput(args)
1475	+	case "listdir":
1476	+		return renderListInput(args)
1477	+	case "grepfiles":
1478	+		return renderGrepInput(args)
1479	+	case "filesearch":
1480	+		return renderFileSearchInput(args)
1481	+	case "websearch":
1482	+		return renderWebSearchInput(args)
1483	+	case "fetchurl":
1484	+		return renderWebFetchInput(args)
1485	+	case "writefile":
1486	+		return renderWriteInput(args)
1487	+	case "editfile":
1488	+		return renderEditInput(args)
1489	+	case "applypatch":
1490	+		return renderApplyPatch(args)
1491	+	case "todowrite", "updateplan", "checklistwrite":
1492	+		return renderTodoWrite(args)
1493	+	case "taskcreate", "taskread", "tasklist", "note":
1494	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1495	+	default:
1496	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1497	+	}
1498	+}
1499	+
1500	+func formatToolOutput(tool *ToolInfo) string {
1501	+	if tool.Output == nil {
1502	+		return ""
1503	+	}
1504	+	content, _ := tool.Output["content"].(string)
1505	+	content = strings.TrimSpace(content)
1506	+	if content == "" {
1507	+		return ""
1508	+	}
1509	+	if strings.Contains(content, "\n") {
1510	+		return fmt.Sprintf("Output:\n```text\n%s\n```", content)
1511	+	}
1512	+	return fmt.Sprintf("Output: %s", content)
1513	+}
1514	+
1515	+// extractPathHints surfaces filesystem paths referenced by a tool's input so
1516	+// downstream features (cloud sync, search) can index sessions by touched
1517	+// files. Mirrors droidcli's extractPathHints — same shell-command extraction
1518	+// via spi.ExtractShellPathHints.
1519	+func extractPathHints(input map[string]interface{}, workspaceRoot string) []string {
1520	+	if input == nil {
1521	+		return nil
1522	+	}
1523	+
1524	+	pathFields := []string{
1525	+		"path", "file_path", "filePath", "file",
1526	+		"dir", "directory", "directory_path", "target_directory",
1527	+		"workdir", "cwd", "target",
1528	+	}
1529	+	var hints []string
1530	+	for _, field := range pathFields {
1531	+		val, ok := input[field]
1532	+		if !ok {
1533	+			continue
1534	+		}
1535	+		switch v := val.(type) {
1536	+		case string:
1537	+			addPathHint(&hints, v, workspaceRoot)
1538	+		case []interface{}:
1539	+			for _, entry := range v {
1540	+				if s, ok := entry.(string); ok {
1541	+					addPathHint(&hints, s, workspaceRoot)
1542	+				}
1543	+			}
1544	+		}
1545	+	}
1546	+
1547	+	command, _ := input["command"].(string)
1548	+	if command == "" {
1549	+		command, _ = input["cmd"].(string)
1550	+	}
1551	+	if command != "" {
1552	+		cwd, _ := input["workdir"].(string)
1553	+		if cwd == "" {
1554	+			cwd, _ = input["cwd"].(string)
1555	+		}
1556	+		if cwd == "" {
1557	+			cwd = workspaceRoot
1558	+		}
1559	+		for _, sp := range spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, cwd, workspaceRoot) {
1560	+			addPathHint(&hints, sp, workspaceRoot)
1561	+		}
1562	+	}
1563	+
1564	+	return hints
1565	+}
1566	+
1567	+func addPathHint(hints *[]string, value string, workspaceRoot string) {
1568	+	value = strings.TrimSpace(value)
1569	+	if value == "" {
1570	+		return
1571	+	}
1572	+	normalized := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
1573	+	for _, existing := range *hints {
1574	+		if existing == normalized {
1575	+			return
1576	+		}
1577	+	}
1578	+	*hints = append(*hints, normalized)
1579	+}
1580	+
1581	+// --- per-tool input renderers ---
1582	+
1583	+func renderReadInput(args map[string]any) string {
1584	+	path := stringValue(args, "file_path", "path", "file", "filePath")
1585	+	if path == "" {
1586	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1587	+	}
1588	+	var b strings.Builder
1589	+	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Path: `%s`", path)
1590	+	offset := stringValue(args, "offset", "start")
1591	+	limit := stringValue(args, "limit", "lines", "max_lines")
1592	+	if offset != "" || limit != "" {
1593	+		if offset == "" {
1594	+			offset = "0"
1595	+		}
1596	+		if limit == "" {
1597	+			limit = "?"
1598	+		}
1599	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\nLines: offset %s, limit %s", offset, limit)
1600	+	}
1601	+	return b.String()
1602	+}
1603	+
1604	+func renderListInput(args map[string]any) string {
1605	+	path := stringValue(args, "path", "dir", "directory", "directory_path", "target_directory")
1606	+	if path == "" {
1607	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1608	+	}
1609	+	return fmt.Sprintf("Path: `%s`", path)
1610	+}
1611	+
1612	+func renderGrepInput(args map[string]any) string {
1613	+	parts := []string{}
1614	+	if pat := stringValue(args, "pattern", "query", "regex"); pat != "" {
1615	+		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Pattern: `%s`", pat))
1616	+	}
1617	+	if path := stringValue(args, "path", "dir", "directory"); path != "" {
1618	+		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Path: `%s`", path))
1619	+	}
1620	+	if glob := stringValue(args, "include", "glob"); glob != "" {
1621	+		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("Glob: `%s`", glob))
1622	+	}
1623	+	if len(parts) == 0 {
1624	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1625	+	}
1626	+	return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1627	+}
1628	+
1629	+func renderFileSearchInput(args map[string]any) string {
1630	+	if pat := stringValue(args, "pattern", "query", "name"); pat != "" {
1631	+		return fmt.Sprintf("Pattern: `%s`", pat)
1632	+	}
1633	+	return renderGenericJSON(args)
1634	+}
1635	+
1636	+func renderWebSearchInput(args map[string]any) string {
1637	+	if q := stringValue(args, "query", "q", "search"); q != "" {
1638	+		return fmt.Sprintf("Query: `%s`", q)
1639	+	}
1640	+	return renderGenericJSON(args)
1641	+}
1642	+
1643	+func renderWebFetchInput(args map[string]any) string {
1644	+	url := stringValue(args, "url", "uri")
1645	+	prompt := stringValue(args, "prompt", "query")
1646	+	if url == "" && prompt == "" {
1647	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1648	+	}
1649	+	var parts []string
1650	+	if url != "" {
1651	+		parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("URL: `%s`", url))
1652	+	}
1653	+	if prompt != "" {
1654	+		parts = append(parts, prompt)
1655	+	}
1656	+	return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
1657	+}
1658	+
1659	+func renderWriteInput(args map[string]any) string {
1660	+	path := stringValue(args, "file_path", "path", "file", "filePath")
1661	+	content := stringValue(args, "content", "contents", "text", "data")
1662	+	if path == "" && content == "" {
1663	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1664	+	}
1665	+	var b strings.Builder
1666	+	if path != "" {
1667	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Path: `%s`", path)
1668	+	}
1669	+	if content != "" {
1670	+		if b.Len() > 0 {
1671	+			b.WriteString("\n\n")
1672	+		}
1673	+		b.WriteString(formatContentBlock(content, path))
1674	+	}
1675	+	return b.String()
1676	+}
1677	+
1678	+func renderEditInput(args map[string]any) string {
1679	+	path := stringValue(args, "file_path", "path", "file", "filePath")
1680	+	oldText := stringValue(args, "old_str", "old_text", "old_string", "old")
1681	+	newText := stringValue(args, "new_str", "new_text", "new_string", "new")
1682	+
1683	+	var b strings.Builder
1684	+	if path != "" {
1685	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Path: `%s`", path)
1686	+	}
1687	+	if oldText != "" || newText != "" {
1688	+		if b.Len() > 0 {
1689	+			b.WriteString("\n\n")
1690	+		}
1691	+		b.WriteString(formatDiffBlock(oldText, newText))
1692	+	}
1693	+	if b.Len() == 0 {
1694	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1695	+	}
1696	+	return b.String()
1697	+}
1698	+
1699	+func renderApplyPatch(args map[string]any) string {
1700	+	patch := stringValue(args, "patch", "input")
1701	+	if patch == "" {
1702	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1703	+	}
1704	+	return fmt.Sprintf("```diff\n%s\n```", strings.TrimSpace(patch))
1705	+}
1706	+
1707	+func renderTodoWrite(args map[string]any) string {
1708	+	itemsRaw, ok := args["todos"].([]interface{})
1709	+	if !ok || len(itemsRaw) == 0 {
1710	+		// Fall back to plan/items keys used by update_plan / checklist_write.
1711	+		for _, key := range []string{"items", "plan", "steps"} {
1712	+			if v, ok := args[key].([]interface{}); ok && len(v) > 0 {
1713	+				itemsRaw = v
1714	+				break
1715	+			}
1716	+		}
1717	+	}
1718	+	if len(itemsRaw) == 0 {
1719	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1720	+	}
1721	+	var b strings.Builder
1722	+	b.WriteString("Todo List:\n")
1723	+	for _, raw := range itemsRaw {
1724	+		item, _ := raw.(map[string]interface{})
1725	+		status := strings.TrimSpace(stringValue(item, "status"))
1726	+		desc := strings.TrimSpace(stringValue(item, "description", "content", "text"))
1727	+		if desc == "" {
1728	+			desc = "(no description)"
1729	+		}
1730	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- [%s] %s\n", todoSymbol(status), desc)
1731	+	}
1732	+	return strings.TrimRight(b.String(), "\n")
1733	+}
1734	+
1735	+func formatExecuteInput(args map[string]any) string {
1736	+	command := stringValue(args, "command", "cmd")
1737	+	workdir := stringValue(args, "workdir", "dir", "cwd")
1738	+	if command == "" && workdir == "" {
1739	+		return renderGenericJSON(args)
1740	+	}
1741	+	var b strings.Builder
1742	+	if workdir != "" {
1743	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Directory: `%s`\n\n", workdir)
1744	+	}
1745	+	if command != "" {
1746	+		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "`%s`", command)
1747	+	}
1748	+	return b.String()
1749	+}
1750	+
1751	+func formatDiffBlock(oldText, newText string) string {
1752	+	var b strings.Builder
1753	+	b.WriteString("```diff\n")
1754	+	for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") {
1755	+		if oldText == "" {
1756	+			break
1757	+		}
1758	+		b.WriteString("-")
1759	+		b.WriteString(line)
1760	+		b.WriteString("\n")
1761	+	}
1762	+	for _, line := range strings.Split(newText, "\n") {
1763	+		if newText == "" {
1764	+			break
1765	+		}
1766	+		b.WriteString("+")
1767	+		b.WriteString(line)
1768	+		b.WriteString("\n")
1769	+	}
1770	+	b.WriteString("```")
1771	+	return b.String()
1772	+}
1773	+
1774	+func formatContentBlock(content, path string) string {
1775	+	lang := languageFromPath(path)
1776	+	escaped := strings.ReplaceAll(content, "```", "\\```")
1777	+	if lang == "" {
1778	+		return fmt.Sprintf("```\n%s\n```", escaped)
1779	+	}
1780	+	return fmt.Sprintf("```%s\n%s\n```", lang, escaped)
1781	+}
1782	+
1783	+func languageFromPath(path string) string {
1784	+	if path == "" {
1785	+		return ""
1786	+	}
1787	+	ext := strings.TrimPrefix(strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(path)), ".")
1788	+	if ext == "" {
1789	+		return ""
1790	+	}
1791	+	switch ext {
1792	+	case "yml":
1793	+		return "yaml"
1794	+	case "md":
1795	+		return "markdown"
1796	+	default:
1797	+		return ext
1798	+	}
1799	+}
1800	+
1801	+// --- generic helpers (decoupled copy of droidcli's, scoped to this package) ---
1802	+
1803	+func renderGenericJSON(args map[string]any) string {
1804	+	if len(args) == 0 {
1805	+		return ""
1806	+	}
1807	+	keys := make([]string, 0, len(args))
1808	+	for k := range args {
1809	+		keys = append(keys, k)
1810	+	}
1811	+	sort.Strings(keys)
1812	+	var b strings.Builder
1813	+	b.WriteString("```json\n{")
1814	+	for i, k := range keys {
1815	+		if i > 0 {
1816	+			b.WriteString(",")
1817	+		}
1818	+		b.WriteString("\n  \"")
1819	+		b.WriteString(k)
1820	+		b.WriteString("\": ")
1821	+		b.WriteString(renderJSONValue(args[k]))
1822	+	}
1823	+	b.WriteString("\n}\n```")
1824	+	return b.String()
1825	+}
1826	+
1827	+func renderJSONValue(v any) string {
1828	+	switch val := v.(type) {
1829	+	case string:
1830	+		bytes, _ := json.Marshal(val)
1831	+		return string(bytes)
1832	+	case json.Number:
1833	+		return val.String()
1834	+	default:
1835	+		bytes, err := json.Marshal(val)
1836	+		if err != nil {
1837	+			return fmt.Sprintf("%q", fmt.Sprint(val))
1838	+		}
1839	+		return string(bytes)
1840	+	}
1841	+}
1842	+
1843	+func stringValue(args map[string]any, keys ...string) string {
1844	+	for _, key := range keys {
1845	+		val, ok := args[key]
1846	+		if !ok {
1847	+			continue
1848	+		}
1849	+		switch v := val.(type) {
1850	+		case string:
1851	+			return v
1852	+		case json.Number:
1853	+			return v.String()
1854	+		case float64:
1855	+			return strconv.FormatFloat(v, 'f', -1, 64)
1856	+		case int:
1857	+			return strconv.Itoa(v)
1858	+		case int64:
1859	+			return strconv.FormatInt(v, 10)
1860	+		case bool:
1861	+			return strconv.FormatBool(v)
1862	+		}
1863	+	}
1864	+	return ""
1865	+}
1866	+
1867	+func normalizeToolName(name string) string {
1868	+	cleaned := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
1869	+	cleaned = strings.ReplaceAll(cleaned, " ", "")
1870	+	cleaned = strings.ReplaceAll(cleaned, "-", "")
1871	+	cleaned = strings.ReplaceAll(cleaned, "_", "")
1872	+	return cleaned
1873	+}
1874	+
1875	+func todoSymbol(status string) string {
1876	+	switch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(status)) {
1877	+	case "completed", "done":
1878	+		return "x"
1879	+	case "in_progress", "active":
1880	+		return "⚡"
1881	+	default:
1882	+		return " "
1883	+	}
1884	+}

1891	+package deepseek
1892	+
1893	+import (
1894	+	"strings"
1895	+	"testing"
1896	+)
1897	+
1898	+// TestFormatToolCall_ReadFile_InputThenOutput exercises the two-phase render
1899	+// pattern: first a tool_use is rendered with input only (no Output map yet),
1900	+// then attachToolResults sets Output and the renderer adds an "Output:" block.
1901	+func TestFormatToolCall_ReadFile_InputThenOutput(t *testing.T) {
1902	+	tool := &ToolInfo{
1903	+		Name:  "read_file",
1904	+		Type:  "read",
1905	+		UseID: "id-1",
1906	+		Input: map[string]interface{}{"file_path": "pkg/main.go"},
1907	+	}
1908	+
1909	+	// Phase 1: input only, no result yet.
1910	+	got := formatToolCall(tool)
1911	+	if !strings.Contains(got, "Path: `pkg/main.go`") {
1912	+		t.Errorf("phase 1 missing input section, got:\n%s", got)
1913	+	}
1914	+	if strings.Contains(got, "Output:") {
1915	+		t.Errorf("phase 1 should not have Output yet, got:\n%s", got)
1916	+	}
1917	+
1918	+	// Phase 2: result attached.
1919	+	tool.Output = map[string]interface{}{"content": "package main\n\nfunc main() {}"}
1920	+	got = formatToolCall(tool)
1921	+	if !strings.Contains(got, "Path: `pkg/main.go`") {
1922	+		t.Errorf("phase 2 lost input section, got:\n%s", got)
1923	+	}
1924	+	if !strings.Contains(got, "Output:") {
1925	+		t.Errorf("phase 2 should now include Output block, got:\n%s", got)
1926	+	}
1927	+	if !strings.Contains(got, "package main") {
1928	+		t.Errorf("phase 2 should include actual content, got:\n%s", got)
1929	+	}
1930	+	// Multi-line output gets fenced with ```text.
1931	+	if !strings.Contains(got, "```text") {
1932	+		t.Errorf("multi-line output should use ```text fence, got:\n%s", got)
1933	+	}
1934	+}
1935	+
1936	+func TestFormatToolCall_NilSafe(t *testing.T) {
1937	+	if got := formatToolCall(nil); got != "" {
1938	+		t.Errorf("expected empty string for nil tool, got %q", got)
1939	+	}
1940	+}
1941	+
1942	+func TestFormatToolOutput(t *testing.T) {
1943	+	tests := []struct {

2239	diff --git a/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseek/path_utils.go b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseek/path_utils.go
2240	new file mode 100644
2241	index 0000000..d1d6f56
2242	--- /dev/null
2243	+++ b/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/deepseek/path_utils.go
2244	@@ -0,0 +1,196 @@
2245	+package deepseek
2246	+
2247	+import (
2248	+	"bufio"
2249	+	"fmt"
2250	+	"os"
2251	+	"path/filepath"
2252	+	"sort"
2253	+	"strings"
2254	+
2255	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
2256	+)
2257	+
2258	+const (
2259	+	deepSeekRootDir     = ".deepseek"
2260	+	deepSeekSessionsDir = "sessions"
2261	+)
2262	+
2263	+type sessionFile struct {
2264	+	Path     string
2265	+	ModTime  int64
2266	+	FileName string
2267	+}
2268	+
2269	+// resolveSessionsDir returns the path to DeepSeek TUI's sessions directory.
2270	+func resolveSessionsDir() (string, error) {
2271	+	home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
2272	+	if err != nil {
2273	+		return "", fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot resolve home dir: %w", err)
2274	+	}
2275	+	return filepath.Join(home, deepSeekRootDir, deepSeekSessionsDir), nil
2276	+}
2277	+
2278	+// listSessionFiles returns all JSON session files in ~/.deepseek/sessions/,
2279	+// sorted by modification time (newest first).
2280	+func listSessionFiles() ([]sessionFile, error) {
2281	+	dir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
2282	+	if err != nil {
2283	+		return nil, err
2284	+	}
2285	+
2286	+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(dir)
2287	+	if err != nil {
2288	+		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
2289	+			return []sessionFile{}, nil
2290	+		}
2291	+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: unable to read sessions dir: %w", err)
2292	+	}
2293	+
2294	+	var files []sessionFile
2295	+	for _, entry := range entries {
2296	+		if entry.IsDir() {
2297	+			continue
2298	+		}
2299	+		if filepath.Ext(entry.Name()) != ".json" {
2300	+			continue
2301	+		}
2302	+
2303	+		info, err := entry.Info()
2304	+		if err != nil {
2305	+			continue
2306	+		}
2307	+
2308	+		files = append(files, sessionFile{
2309	+			Path:     filepath.Join(dir, entry.Name()),
2310	+			ModTime:  info.ModTime().UnixNano(),
2311	+			FileName: entry.Name(),
2312	+		})
2313	+	}
2314	+
2315	+	// Sort newest first.
2316	+	sort.Slice(files, func(i, j int) bool {
2317	+		return files[i].ModTime > files[j].ModTime
2318	+	})
2319	+
2320	+	return files, nil
2321	+}
2322	+
2323	+// findSessionFileByID finds a session file by its ID (UUID).
2324	+// The ID should match a filename like <uuid>.json.
2325	+func findSessionFileByID(sessionID string) (string, error) {
2326	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
2327	+	if err != nil {
2328	+		return "", err
2329	+	}
2330	+
2331	+	for _, file := range files {
2332	+		candidate := trimExt(file.FileName)
2333	+		if candidate == sessionID || file.FileName == sessionID {
2334	+			return file.Path, nil
2335	+		}
2336	+	}
2337	+	return "", nil
2338	+}
2339	+
2340	+func trimExt(name string) string {
2341	+	ext := filepath.Ext(name)
2342	+	if ext == "" {
2343	+		return name
2344	+	}
2345	+	return name[:len(name)-len(ext)]
2346	+}
2347	+
2348	+// sessionMentionsProject checks whether a session file's workspace metadata
2349	+// matches the given project path.
2350	+func sessionMentionsProject(filePath string, projectPath string) bool {
2351	+	projectPath = strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
2352	+	if projectPath == "" {
2353	+		return false
2354	+	}
2355	+
2356	+	// Quick check: read only the metadata portion of the JSON to avoid
2357	+	// parsing the full messages array.
2358	+	workspace := extractWorkspaceFast(filePath)
2359	+	if workspace == "" {
2360	+		return false
2361	+	}
2362	+
2363	+	canonicalProject := canonicalizePath(projectPath)
2364	+	canonicalWorkspace := canonicalizePath(workspace)
2365	+
2366	+	return canonicalProject == canonicalWorkspace
2367	+}
2368	+
2369	+// extractWorkspaceFast reads just the workspace field from a session file
2370	+// without parsing the full JSON. The metadata block is at the top of the file,
2371	+// so we stream and bail on the first match — important on the watcher hot
2372	+// path where this is called per fs event.
2373	+func extractWorkspaceFast(filePath string) string {
2374	+	f, err := os.Open(filePath)
2375	+	if err != nil {
2376	+		return ""
2377	+	}
2378	+	defer func() { _ = f.Close() }()
2379	+
2380	+	scanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)
2381	+	// DeepSeek session files can contain individual lines well over the default
2382	+	// 64KB scanner limit (long tool_result blobs); raise to 1MB which still
2383	+	// bounds memory but covers everything seen in practice.
2384	+	scanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64*1024), 1024*1024)
2385	+	for scanner.Scan() {
2386	+		line := scanner.Text()
2387	+		messagesIdx := strings.Index(line, `"messages"`)
2388	+		searchLine := line
2389	+		if messagesIdx >= 0 {
2390	+			searchLine = line[:messagesIdx]
2391	+		}
2392	+		if ws := matchWorkspace(searchLine); ws != "" {
2393	+			return ws
2394	+		}
2395	+		// Once we cross into the messages array the metadata is behind us.
2396	+		if messagesIdx >= 0 {
2397	+			return ""
2398	+		}
2399	+	}
2400	+	return ""
2401	+}
2402	+
2403	+// matchWorkspace pulls the value out of a `"workspace": "<value>"` JSON line.
2404	+func matchWorkspace(line string) string {
2405	+	idx := strings.Index(line, `"workspace"`)
2406	+	if idx < 0 {
2407	+		return ""
2408	+	}
2409	+	rest := line[idx+len(`"workspace"`):]
2410	+	colon := strings.Index(rest, ":")
2411	+	if colon < 0 {
2412	+		return ""
2413	+	}
2414	+	rest = strings.TrimSpace(rest[colon+1:])
2415	+	if len(rest) == 0 || rest[0] != '"' {
2416	+		return ""
2417	+	}
2418	+	rest = rest[1:]
2419	+	end := strings.Index(rest, `"`)
2420	+	if end < 0 {
2421	+		return ""
2422	+	}
2423	+	return rest[:end]
2424	+}
2425	+
2426	+// canonicalizePath resolves a path to its canonical form for comparison.
2427	+func canonicalizePath(path string) string {
2428	+	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(path)
2429	+	if trimmed == "" {
2430	+		return ""
2431	+	}
2432	+	canonical, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(trimmed)
2433	+	if err == nil {
2434	+		return canonical
2435	+	}
2436	+	if abs, err := filepath.Abs(trimmed); err == nil {
2437	+		return filepath.Clean(abs)
2438	+	}
2439	+	return filepath.Clean(trimmed)
2440	+}

2456	+// TestExtractWorkspaceFast covers the streaming workspace extraction. The
2457	+// function must:
2458	+//  1. Return the workspace value when it appears in metadata before "messages".
2459	+//  2. NOT pick up "workspace" strings that appear inside tool_result content
2460	+//     after the "messages" key has been seen — those are unrelated noise.
2461	+//  3. Return "" when no workspace is present at all.
2462	+//  4. Handle very large lines (the buffer was raised to 1MB for tool blobs).
2463	+func TestExtractWorkspaceFast(t *testing.T) {
2464	+	tests := []struct {
2465	+		name    string
2466	+		content string
2467	+		want    string
2468	+	}{
2469	+		{
2470	+			name: "workspace before messages key",
2471	+			content: `{
2472	+  "schema_version": 1,
2473	+  "metadata": {
2474	+    "id": "sess-1",
2475	+    "workspace": "/Users/me/proj"
2476	+  },
2477	+  "messages": []
2478	+}`,
2479	+			want: "/Users/me/proj",
2480	+		},
2481	+		{
2482	+			name: "workspace key only inside tool_result after messages is ignored",
2483	+			content: `{
2484	+  "metadata": {"id": "sess-1"},
2485	+  "messages": [
2486	+    {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "tool_result", "content": "this string mentions \"workspace\": \"/fake\""}]}
2487	+  ]
2488	+}`,
2489	+			want: "",
2490	+		},
2491	+		{
2492	+			name: "no workspace key at all",
2493	+			content: `{
2494	+  "metadata": {"id": "sess-2"},
2495	+  "messages": []
2496	+}`,
2497	+			want: "",
2498	+		},
2499	+		{
2500	+			name: "workspace on same line as messages key still found if it appears first",
2501	+			// matchWorkspace runs before the messages bail-out, so a single-line
2502	+			// JSON with workspace before messages should still resolve.
2503	+			content: `{"metadata":{"workspace":"/single/line/path"},"messages":[]}`,
2504	+			want:    "/single/line/path",
2505	+		},
2506	+		{
2507	+			name:    "workspace-like text after messages on same line is ignored",
2508	+			content: `{"metadata":{"id":"sess-1"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","content":"this string mentions \"workspace\": \"/fake\""}]}]}`,
2509	+			want:    "",
2510	+		},
2511	+		{
2512	+			name: "empty workspace value yields empty result",
2513	+			content: `{
2514	+  "metadata": {"workspace": ""},
2515	+  "messages": []
2516	+}`,
2517	+			want: "",
2518	+		},
2519	+	}
2520	+
2521	+	for _, tt := range tests {
2522	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
2523	+			path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "session.json")
2524	+			if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(tt.content), 0o644); err != nil {
2525	+				t.Fatalf("write temp file: %v", err)
2526	+			}
2527	+			got := extractWorkspaceFast(path)
2528	+			if got != tt.want {
2529	+				t.Errorf("extractWorkspaceFast() = %q, want %q", got, tt.want)
2530	+			}
2531	+		})
2532	+	}
2533	+}
2534	+
2535	+// TestExtractWorkspaceFast_LargeLine confirms the scanner's 1MB buffer handles
2536	+// tool_result blobs that exceed bufio.Scanner's default 64KB line cap. Without
2537	+// the raise, the scanner would return an error and the workspace (which sits
2538	+// before the giant line) would have been read fine — but if the workspace
2539	+// itself were on a long metadata line we'd lose it. Construct a case where the
2540	+// workspace lives on a long line to exercise the buffer.
2541	+func TestExtractWorkspaceFast_LargeLine(t *testing.T) {
2542	+	// Build a metadata line that's ~200KB long but still valid JSON parseable
2543	+	// by our quick matcher (we only scan textually).
2544	+	bigComment := strings.Repeat("x", 200*1024)
2545	+	content := `{"metadata":{"note":"` + bigComment + `","workspace":"/big/line/path"},"messages":[]}`
2546	+
2547	+	path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "session.json")
2548	+	if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
2549	+		t.Fatalf("write temp file: %v", err)
2550	+	}
2551	+	got := extractWorkspaceFast(path)
2552	+	if got != "/big/line/path" {
2553	+		t.Errorf("extractWorkspaceFast() = %q, want /big/line/path", got)
2554	+	}
2555	+}
2556	+
2557	+// TestExtractWorkspaceFast_MissingFile returns empty string instead of erroring.
2558	+func TestExtractWorkspaceFast_MissingFile(t *testing.T) {
2559	+	got := extractWorkspaceFast(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "no-such-file.json"))
2560	+	if got != "" {
2561	+		t.Errorf("expected empty string for missing file, got %q", got)
2562	+	}
2563	+}
2564	+
2565	+func TestSessionMentionsProject(t *testing.T) {
2566	+	tests := []struct {
2567	+		name        string
2568	+		makeContent func(projectDir, otherDir string) string
2569	+		projectDir  func(projectDir, otherDir string) string
2570	+		want        bool
2571	+	}{
2572	+		{
2573	+			name: "matches when workspace equals project",
2574	+			makeContent: func(p, _ string) string {
2575	+				return `{"metadata":{"workspace":"` + p + `"},"messages":[]}`
2576	+			},
2577	+			projectDir: func(p, _ string) string { return p },
2578	+			want:       true,
2579	+		},
2580	+		{
2581	+			name: "no match when workspace differs",
2582	+			makeContent: func(_, o string) string {
2583	+				return `{"metadata":{"workspace":"` + o + `"},"messages":[]}`
2584	+			},
2585	+			projectDir: func(p, _ string) string { return p },
2586	+			want:       false,
2587	+		},
2588	+		{
2589	+			name: "no match when no workspace key at all",
2590	+			makeContent: func(_, _ string) string {
2591	+				return `{"metadata":{"id":"x"},"messages":[]}`
2592	+			},
2593	+			projectDir: func(p, _ string) string { return p },
2594	+			want:       false,
2595	+		},
2596	+		{
2597	+			name: "empty project path returns false",
2598	+			makeContent: func(p, _ string) string {
2599	+				return `{"metadata":{"workspace":"` + p + `"},"messages":[]}`
2600	+			},
2601	+			projectDir: func(_, _ string) string { return "   " },
2602	+			want:       false,
2603	+		},
2604	+	}
2605	+
2606	+	for _, tt := range tests {
2607	+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
2608	+			projectDir := t.TempDir()
2609	+			otherDir := t.TempDir()
2610	+			path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "session.json")
2611	+			if err := os.WriteFile(path, []byte(tt.makeContent(projectDir, otherDir)), 0o644); err != nil {
2612	+				t.Fatalf("write file: %v", err)
2613	+			}
2614	+			got := sessionMentionsProject(path, tt.projectDir(projectDir, otherDir))
2615	+			if got != tt.want {
2616	+				t.Errorf("sessionMentionsProject() = %v, want %v", got, tt.want)
2617	+			}
2618	+		})
2619	+	}
2620	+}
2621	+
2622	+func TestCanonicalizePath(t *testing.T) {
2623	+	// Use real temp dirs so canonicalization (which calls filepath.EvalSymlinks
2624	+	// under the hood) can resolve them. We can't assert exact strings on macOS
2625	+	// because /var → /private/var, so we assert that equivalent inputs produce
2626	+	// equivalent outputs and that empty input returns empty.
2627	+	t.Run("empty input returns empty", func(t *testing.T) {
2628	+		if got := canonicalizePath(""); got != "" {
2629	+			t.Errorf("expected empty, got %q", got)
2630	+		}
2631	+		if got := canonicalizePath("   "); got != "" {
2632	+			t.Errorf("expected empty for whitespace, got %q", got)
2633	+		}
2634	+	})
2635	+
2636	+	t.Run("equivalent paths canonicalize to same value", func(t *testing.T) {
2637	+		dir := t.TempDir()
2638	+		// Same dir referenced two ways must canonicalize identically.
2639	+		canonical := canonicalizePath(dir)
2640	+		canonicalAgain := canonicalizePath(dir + "/")
2641	+		if canonical != canonicalAgain {
2642	+			t.Errorf("trailing slash changed canonical form: %q vs %q", canonical, canonicalAgain)
2643	+		}
2644	+		if canonical == "" {
2645	+			t.Errorf("expected non-empty canonical for %q", dir)
2646	+		}
2647	+	})
2648	+
2649	+	t.Run("nonexistent path still canonicalizes to absolute form", func(t *testing.T) {
2650	+		got := canonicalizePath("/this/path/definitely/does/not/exist/xyz")
2651	+		if !filepath.IsAbs(got) {
2652	+			t.Errorf("expected absolute path for nonexistent input, got %q", got)
2653	+		}
2654	+	})
2655	+}

2662	+package deepseek
2663	+
2664	+import (
2665	+	"bytes"

2673	+

2676	+	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
2677	+)

2679	+const (
2680	+	defaultCommand = "deepseek"
2681	+	versionFlag    = "--version"
2682	+)
2683	+
2684	+// Provider implements the spi.Provider interface for DeepSeek TUI.
2685	+type Provider struct{}
2686	+
2687	+// NewProvider creates a new DeepSeek TUI provider instance.
2688	+func NewProvider() *Provider {
2689	+	return &Provider{}
2690	+}
2691	+
2692	+func (p *Provider) Name() string {
2693	+	return "DeepSeek TUI"
2694	+}
2695	+
2696	+// Check verifies that DeepSeek TUI is available and returns its resolved
2697	+// location and version information.
2698	+func (p *Provider) Check(customCommand string) spi.CheckResult {
2699	+	cmdName, _ := parseCommand(customCommand)
2700	+	isCustom := strings.TrimSpace(customCommand) != ""
2701	+
2702	+	resolved, err := exec.LookPath(cmdName)
2703	+	if err != nil {
2704	+		msg := buildCheckErrorMessage("not_found", cmdName, isCustom, "")
2705	+		trackCheckFailure("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, "", versionFlag, "", "not_found", err.Error())
2706	+		return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: "", ErrorMessage: msg}
2707	+	}
2708	+
2709	+	var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
2710	+	cmd := exec.Command(resolved, versionFlag)
2711	+	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
2712	+	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
2713	+	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
2714	+		errorType := classifyCheckError(err)
2715	+		stderrOutput := strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String())
2716	+		msg := buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType, resolved, isCustom, stderrOutput)
2717	+		trackCheckFailure("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, versionFlag, stderrOutput, errorType, err.Error())
2718	+		return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: resolved, ErrorMessage: msg}
2719	+	}
2720	+
2721	+	version := strings.TrimSpace(stdout.String())
2722	+	if version == "" {
2723	+		version = "unknown"
2724	+	}
2725	+	trackCheckSuccess("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, version, versionFlag)
2726	+	return spi.CheckResult{Success: true, Version: version, Location: resolved}
2727	+}
2728	+
2729	+// DetectAgent reports whether DeepSeek TUI has been used in the given project
2730	+// based on existing session files in ~/.deepseek/sessions/.
2731	+func (p *Provider) DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool {
2732	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
2733	+	if err != nil {
2734	+		if helpOutput {
2735	+			log.UserWarn("DeepSeek TUI detection failed: %v", err)
2736	+		}
2737	+		return false
2738	+	}
2739	+	if len(files) == 0 {
2740	+		if helpOutput {
2741	+			printDetectionHelp()
2742	+		}
2743	+		return false
2744	+	}
2745	+	if projectPath == "" {
2746	+		return true
2747	+	}
2748	+	for _, file := range files {
2749	+		if sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
2750	+			return true
2751	+		}
2752	+	}
2753	+	if helpOutput {
2754	+		printDetectionHelp()
2755	+	}
2756	+	return false
2757	+}
2758	+
2759	+// GetAgentChatSessions returns all available agent chat sessions from DeepSeek TUI,
2760	+// optionally filtered to sessions associated with the given projectPath.
2761	+func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
2762	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
2763	+	if err != nil {
2764	+		return nil, err
2765	+	}
2766	+
2767	+	normalizedProject := strings.TrimSpace(projectPath)
2768	+
2769	+	var matchingFiles []sessionFile
2770	+	for _, file := range files {
2771	+		if normalizedProject != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, normalizedProject) {
2772	+			continue
2773	+		}
2774	+		matchingFiles = append(matchingFiles, file)
2775	+	}
2776	+
2777	+	totalFiles := len(matchingFiles)
2778	+	result := make([]spi.AgentChatSession, 0, len(matchingFiles))
2779	+
2780	+	for i, file := range matchingFiles {
2781	+		session, err := parseSessionFile(file.Path)
2782	+		if err != nil {
2783	+			slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
2784	+			if progress != nil {
2785	+				progress(i+1, totalFiles)
2786	+			}
2787	+			continue
2788	+		}
2789	+		chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
2790	+		if chat != nil {
2791	+			result = append(result, *chat)
2792	+		}
2793	+		if progress != nil {
2794	+			progress(i+1, totalFiles)
2795	+		}
2796	+	}
2797	+	return result, nil
2798	+}
2799	+

2816	+// ExecAgentAndWatch executes DeepSeek TUI for the given project and watches
2817	+// for session updates.
2818	+func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
2819	+	if sessionCallback == nil {
2820	+		return ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
2821	+	}
2822	+
2823	+	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
2824	+	defer cancel()
2825	+
2826	+	watchErr := make(chan error, 1)
2827	+	go func() {
2828	+		watchErr <- watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
2829	+	}()
2830	+
2831	+	err := ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
2832	+	cancel()
2833	+
2834	+	if werr := <-watchErr; werr != nil && !errors.Is(werr, context.Canceled) {
2835	+		slog.Warn("deepseek: watcher stopped with error", "error", werr)
2836	+	}
2837	+
2838	+	if err != nil {
2839	+		return fmt.Errorf("deepseek execution failed: %w", err)
2840	+	}
2841	+	return nil
2842	+}
2843	+
2844	+// WatchAgent monitors DeepSeek TUI sessions for the given project and invokes
2845	+// sessionCallback for each new or updated session until ctx is canceled.
2846	+func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
2847	+	if sessionCallback == nil {
2848	+		return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
2849	+	}
2850	+	return watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
2851	+}

2883	+// --- helpers shared across the package ---
2884	+
2885	+func classifyCheckError(err error) string {
2886	+	var execErr *exec.Error
2887	+	var pathErr *os.PathError
2888	+	switch {
2889	+	case errors.As(err, &execErr) && execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound:
2890	+		return "not_found"
2891	+	case errors.As(err, &pathErr) && errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrPermission):
2892	+		return "permission_denied"
2893	+	case errors.Is(err, os.ErrPermission):
2894	+		return "permission_denied"
2895	+	default:
2896	+		return "version_failed"
2897	+	}
2898	+}
2899	+
2900	+func buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType string, command string, isCustom bool, stderr string) string {
2901	+	var builder strings.Builder
2902	+	switch errorType {
2903	+	case "not_found":
2904	+		builder.WriteString("DeepSeek TUI was not found.\n\n")
2905	+		if isCustom {
2906	+			builder.WriteString("• Verify the custom path you provided is executable.\n")
2907	+			fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "• Provided command: %s\n", command)
2908	+		} else {
2909	+			builder.WriteString("• Install DeepSeek TUI and ensure `deepseek` is on your PATH.\n")
2910	+			builder.WriteString("• Re-run `specstory check deepseek` after installation.\n")
2911	+		}
2912	+	case "permission_denied":
2913	+		builder.WriteString("SpecStory cannot execute DeepSeek TUI due to permissions.\n\n")
2914	+		fmt.Fprintf(&builder, "Try: chmod +x %s\n", command)
2915	+	default:
2916	+		builder.WriteString("`deepseek --version` failed.\n\n")
2917	+		if stderr != "" {
2918	+			builder.WriteString("Error output:\n")
2919	+			builder.WriteString(stderr)
2920	+			builder.WriteString("\n\n")
2921	+		}
2922	+		builder.WriteString("Run `deepseek --version` manually to diagnose, then retry.")
2923	+	}
2924	+	return builder.String()
2925	+}
2926	+
2927	+func trackCheckSuccess(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, version string, versionFlag string) {
2928	+	props := analytics.Properties{
2929	+		"provider":       provider,
2930	+		"custom_command": custom,
2931	+		"command_path":   commandPath,
2932	+		"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
2933	+		"version":        version,
2934	+		"version_flag":   versionFlag,
2935	+	}
2936	+	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallSuccess, props)
2937	+}
2938	+
2939	+func trackCheckFailure(provider string, custom bool, commandPath string, resolvedPath string, versionFlag string, stderrOutput string, errorType string, message string) {
2940	+	props := analytics.Properties{
2941	+		"provider":       provider,
2942	+		"custom_command": custom,
2943	+		"command_path":   commandPath,
2944	+		"resolved_path":  resolvedPath,
2945	+		"version_flag":   versionFlag,
2946	+		"error_type":     errorType,
2947	+		"error_message":  message,
2948	+	}
2949	+	if stderrOutput != "" {
2950	+		props["stderr"] = stderrOutput
2951	+	}
2952	+	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, props)

3081	+func TestDetectAgent_NoSessionsReturnsFalse(t *testing.T) {
3082	+	// When ~/.deepseek/sessions doesn't exist (or is empty), DetectAgent must
3083	+	// return false without panicking. This guards against regressions where
3084	+	// listSessionFiles starts erroring on missing dirs.
3085	+	tmp := t.TempDir()
3086	+	t.Setenv("HOME", tmp)
3087	+
3088	+	p := NewProvider()
3089	+	if got := p.DetectAgent("/some/project", false); got {
3090	+		t.Errorf("DetectAgent on empty home = true, want false")
3091	+	}
3092	+}

3094	+func TestDetectAgent_EmptyProjectMatchesAnySession(t *testing.T) {
3095	+	// An empty projectPath means "do you have any sessions at all?" — if yes,

3106	+	}

3119	+package deepseek
3120	+
3121	+import (
3122	+	"context"
3123	+	"fmt"
