```text
3132	+)
3133	+
3134	+type watchState struct {
3135	+	lastProcessed map[string]int64
3136	+}
3137	+
3138	+// watchSessions watches the DeepSeek TUI sessions directory for new or modified
3139	+// session files and invokes sessionCallback for each change.
3140	+func watchSessions(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
3141	+	if sessionCallback == nil {
3142	+		return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
3143	+	}
3144	+
3145	+	sessionsDir, err := resolveSessionsDir()
3146	+	if err != nil {
3147	+		return err
3148	+	}
3149	+
3150	+	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
3151	+	if err != nil {
3152	+		return fmt.Errorf("deepseek: failed to create watcher: %w", err)
3153	+	}
3154	+	defer func() {
3155	+		_ = watcher.Close()
3156	+	}()
3157	+
3158	+	state := &watchState{lastProcessed: make(map[string]int64)}
3159	+
3160	+	// Initial scan to process existing sessions.
3161	+	if err := scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state); err != nil {
3162	+		slog.Debug("deepseek: initial scan failed", "error", err)
3163	+	}
3164	+
3165	+	// Set up watching on the sessions directory.
3166	+	if err := setupWatcher(watcher, sessionsDir); err != nil {
3167	+		slog.Debug("deepseek: setup watches failed", "error", err)
3168	+	}
3169	+
3170	+	// Event loop.
3171	+	for {
3172	+		select {
3173	+		case <-ctx.Done():
3174	+			return ctx.Err()
3175	+		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
3176	+			if !ok {
3177	+				return nil
3178	+			}
3179	+			handleWatchEvent(event, watcher, sessionsDir, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
3180	+		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
3181	+			if !ok {
3182	+				return nil
3183	+			}
3184	+			slog.Debug("deepseek: watcher error", "error", err)
3185	+		}
3186	+	}
3187	+}
3188	+
3189	+// setupWatcher adds a watch on the sessions directory.
3190	+// If the directory doesn't exist yet, it watches the parent .deepseek directory
3191	+// so we can detect when sessions/ is created.
3192	+func setupWatcher(watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string) error {
3193	+	if _, err := os.Stat(sessionsDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
3194	+		// Watch parent .deepseek directory.
3195	+		parentDir := filepath.Dir(sessionsDir)
3196	+		if _, err := os.Stat(parentDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
3197	+			homeDir := filepath.Dir(parentDir)
3198	+			slog.Debug("deepseek: watching home for .deepseek creation", "path", homeDir)
3199	+			return watcher.Add(homeDir)
3200	+		}
3201	+		slog.Debug("deepseek: watching .deepseek for sessions creation", "path", parentDir)
3202	+		return watcher.Add(parentDir)
3203	+	}
3204	+
3205	+	slog.Debug("deepseek: watching sessions directory", "path", sessionsDir)
3206	+	return watcher.Add(sessionsDir)
3207	+}
3208	+
3209	+// handleWatchEvent processes a filesystem event for new or modified session files.
3210	+func handleWatchEvent(event fsnotify.Event, watcher *fsnotify.Watcher, sessionsDir string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
3211	+	slog.Debug("deepseek: received fs event", "op", event.Op.String(), "path", event.Name)
3212	+
3213	+	// Handle directory creation — add new watches.
3214	+	if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
3215	+		info, err := os.Stat(event.Name)
3216	+		if err != nil {
3217	+			return
3218	+		}
3219	+		if info.IsDir() {
3220	+			// If sessions dir itself was just created, set up proper watch.
3221	+			if event.Name == sessionsDir {
3222	+				if err := setupWatcher(watcher, sessionsDir); err != nil {
3223	+					slog.Debug("deepseek: failed to setup watch on new sessions dir", "error", err)
3224	+				}
3225	+				// Scan immediately for any pre-existing files.
3226	+				if scanErr := scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state); scanErr != nil {
3227	+					slog.Debug("deepseek: scan after new dir watch failed", "error", scanErr)
3228	+				}
3229	+			}
3230	+			return
3231	+		}
3232	+	}
3233	+
3234	+	// Only process .json files.
3235	+	if !strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".json") {
3236	+		return
3237	+	}
3238	+
3239	+	// Process on Create and Write events.
3240	+	if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
3241	+		return
3242	+	}
3243	+
3244	+	slog.Debug("deepseek: json file event", "op", event.Op.String(), "file", event.Name)
3245	+	processSessionFile(event.Name, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback, state)
3246	+}
3247	+
3248	+// processSessionFile processes a single JSON session file if it has been modified.
3249	+func processSessionFile(filePath string, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) {
3250	+	info, err := os.Stat(filePath)
3251	+	if err != nil {
3252	+		return
3253	+	}
3254	+
3255	+	modTime := info.ModTime().UnixNano()
3256	+	if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[filePath]; ok && last >= modTime {
3257	+		return
3258	+	}
3259	+
3260	+	if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(filePath, projectPath) {
3261	+		return
3262	+	}
3263	+
3264	+	session, err := parseSessionFile(filePath)
3265	+	if err != nil {
3266	+		slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session", "path", filePath, "error", err)
3267	+		return
3268	+	}
3269	+
3270	+	chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
3271	+	if chat == nil {
3272	+		return
3273	+	}
3274	+
3275	+	state.lastProcessed[filePath] = modTime
3276	+	dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
3277	+}
3278	+
3279	+// scanAndProcessSessions scans all session files and processes any that have been modified.
3280	+func scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) error {
3281	+	files, err := listSessionFiles()
3282	+	if err != nil {
3283	+		return err
3284	+	}
3285	+
3286	+	for _, file := range files {
3287	+		if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[file.Path]; ok && last >= file.ModTime {
3288	+			continue
3289	+		}
3290	+		if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
3291	+			continue
3292	+		}
3293	+		session, err := parseSessionFile(file.Path)
3294	+		if err != nil {
3295	+			slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
3296	+			continue
3297	+		}
3298	+		chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
3299	+		if chat == nil {
3300	+			continue
3301	+		}
3302	+		state.lastProcessed[file.Path] = file.ModTime
3303	+		dispatchSession(sessionCallback, chat)
3304	+	}
3305	+	return nil
3306	+}
3307	+
3308	+// dispatchSession invokes the session callback in a goroutine with panic recovery.
3309	+func dispatchSession(sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), session *spi.AgentChatSession) {
3310	+	if sessionCallback == nil || session == nil {
3311	+		return
3312	+	}
3313	+	go func() {
3314	+		defer func() {
3315	+			if r := recover(); r != nil {
3316	+				slog.Error("deepseek: session callback panicked", "panic", r)
3317	+			}
3318	+		}()
3319	+		sessionCallback(session)
3320	+	}()
3321	+}
```

```text
353		var hints []string
354		if input == nil {
355			return hints
356		}

358			val, ok := input[field]
359			if !ok {
360				continue
361			}

363			case string:
364				addPathHint(&hints, v, workspaceRoot)
365			case []interface{}:
366				for _, entry := range v {
367					if s, ok := entry.(string); ok {
368						addPathHint(&hints, s, workspaceRoot)
369					}
370				}
371			case json.Number:
372				addPathHint(&hints, v.String(), workspaceRoot)
373			}
374		}
375	
376		// Extract paths from shell commands (redirect targets, file-creating commands)
377		// Check both "command" and "cmd" field names for shell tools
378		command, _ := input["command"].(string)
379		if command == "" {
380			command, _ = input["cmd"].(string)
381		}
382		if command != "" {
383			cwd, _ := input["workdir"].(string)
384			if cwd == "" {
385				cwd, _ = input["cwd"].(string)
386			}
387			if cwd == "" {
388				cwd = workspaceRoot
389			}
390			shellPaths := spi.ExtractShellPathHints(command, cwd, workspaceRoot)
391			for _, sp := range shellPaths {
392				addPathHint(&hints, sp, workspaceRoot)
393			}
394		}
395	
396		return hints
397	}
398	
399	func addPathHint(hints *[]string, value string, workspaceRoot string) {
400		value = strings.TrimSpace(value)
401		if value == "" {
402			return
403		}
404		normalized := spi.NormalizePath(value, workspaceRoot)
405		for _, existing := range *hints {
406			if existing == normalized {
407				return
408			}
409		}
410		*hints = append(*hints, normalized)
411	}
412
```
</details></tool-use>

554	// parseAskUserAnswers extracts answers from the Factory Droid AskUser result format.
555	// Format: "1. [question] Text\n[answer] Answer1\n\n2. [question] Text\n[answer] Answer2"
556	func parseAskUserAnswers(text string) []string {
557		var answers []string
558		lines := strings.Split(text, "\n")
559		for _, line := range lines {
560			line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
561			if strings.HasPrefix(line, "[answer]") {
562				answer := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimPrefix(line, "[answer]"))
563				if answer != "" {
564					answers = append(answers, answer)
565				}
566			}
567		}
568		return answers
569	}
570	
571	func renderGenericJSON(args map[string]any) string {
572		if len(args) == 0 {
573			return ""
574		}
575		ordered := make([]string, 0, len(args))
576		for key := range args {
577			ordered = append(ordered, key)
578		}
579		sort.Strings(ordered)
580		var builder strings.Builder
581		builder.WriteString("```json\n{")
582		for idx, key := range ordered {
583			if idx > 0 {
584				builder.WriteString(",")
585			}
586			builder.WriteString("\n  \"")
587			builder.WriteString(key)
588			builder.WriteString("\": ")
589			builder.WriteString(renderJSONValue(args[key]))
590		}
591		builder.WriteString("\n}\n```")
592		return builder.String()
593	}
594	
595	func renderJSONValue(v any) string {
596		switch val := v.(type) {
597		case string:
598			bytes, _ := json.Marshal(val)
599			return string(bytes)
600		case json.Number:
601			return val.String()
602		case []byte:
603			bytes, _ := json.Marshal(string(val))
604			return string(bytes)
605		default:
606			bytes, err := json.Marshal(val)
607			if err != nil {
608				return fmt.Sprintf("\"%s\"", fmt.Sprint(val))
609			}
610			return string(bytes)
611		}
612	}
613	
614	func stringValue(args map[string]any, keys ...string) string {
615		for _, key := range keys {
616			if val, ok := args[key]; ok {
617				switch v := val.(type) {
618				case string:
619					return v
620				case json.Number:
621					return v.String()
622				case fmt.Stringer:
623					return v.String()
624				case []byte:
625					return string(v)
626				case float64:
627					return strconv.FormatFloat(v, 'f', -1, 64)
628				case float32:
629					return strconv.FormatFloat(float64(v), 'f', -1, 32)
630				case int:
631					return strconv.Itoa(v)
632				case int64:
633					return strconv.FormatInt(v, 10)
634				case int32:
635					return strconv.FormatInt(int64(v), 10)
636				case uint:
637					return strconv.FormatUint(uint64(v), 10)
638				case uint64:
639					return strconv.FormatUint(v, 10)
640				case bool:
641					return strconv.FormatBool(v)
642				}
643			}
644		}
645		return ""
646	}
647	
648	func stringSliceValue(args map[string]any, keys ...string) []string {

654				case []interface{}:

656					for _, entry := range v {

```text
30	// filterWarmupMessages removes warmup messages (sidechain messages before first real message)
31	func filterWarmupMessages(records []JSONLRecord) []JSONLRecord {
32		// Find first non-sidechain message
33		firstRealMessageIndex := -1
34		for i, record := range records {
35			if isSidechain, ok := record.Data["isSidechain"].(bool); !ok || !isSidechain {
36				firstRealMessageIndex = i
37				break
38			}
39		}
40	
41		// No real messages found
42		if firstRealMessageIndex == -1 {
43			return []JSONLRecord{}
44		}
45	
46		// Return records starting from first real message
47		return records[firstRealMessageIndex:]
48	}
49	
50	// processSession filters warmup messages and converts a Session to an AgentChatSession
51	// Returns nil if the session is warmup-only (no real messages)
52	func processSession(session Session, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
53		// Write debug files first (even for warmup-only sessions)
54		if debugRaw {
55			// Write debug files and get the record-to-file mapping
56			_ = writeDebugRawFiles(session) // Unused but needed for side effect
57		}
58	
59		// Filter warmup messages
60		filteredRecords := filterWarmupMessages(session.Records)
61	
62		// Skip if no real messages remain after filtering warmup
63		if len(filteredRecords) == 0 {
64			slog.Debug("Skipping warmup-only session", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid)
65			return nil
66		}
67	
68		// Create session with filtered records
69		filteredSession := Session{
70			SessionUuid: session.SessionUuid,
71			Records:     filteredRecords,
72		}
73	
74		// Get timestamp from root record
75		rootRecord := filteredSession.Records[0]
76		timestamp := rootRecord.Data["timestamp"].(string)
77	
78		// Get the slug
79		slug := FileSlugFromRootRecord(filteredSession)
80
```

```text
110	}

112	// buildCheckErrorMessage creates a user-facing error message tailored to the failure type.
113	func buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType string, claudeCmd string, isCustom bool, stderrOutput string) string {
114		var errorMsg strings.Builder
115	
116		switch errorType {
117		case "not_found":
118			fmt.Fprintf(&errorMsg, "  🔍 Could not find Claude Code at: %s\n", claudeCmd)
119			errorMsg.WriteString("\n")
120			errorMsg.WriteString("  💡 Here's how to fix this:\n")
121			errorMsg.WriteString("\n")
122			if isCustom {
123				errorMsg.WriteString("     The specified path doesn't exist. Please check:\n")
124				fmt.Fprintf(&errorMsg, "     • Is Claude Code installed at %s?\n", claudeCmd)
125				errorMsg.WriteString("     • Did you type the path correctly?")
126			} else {
127				errorMsg.WriteString("     1. Make sure Claude Code is installed:\n")
128				errorMsg.WriteString("        • Visit https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/quickstart\n")
129				errorMsg.WriteString("\n")
130				errorMsg.WriteString("     2. If it's already installed, try:\n")
131				errorMsg.WriteString("        • Check if 'claude' is in your PATH\n")
132				errorMsg.WriteString("        • Use -c flag to specify the full path\n")
133				errorMsg.WriteString("        • Example: specstory check claude -c \"~/.claude/local/claude\"")
134			}
135		case "permission_denied":
136			fmt.Fprintf(&errorMsg, "  🔒 Permission denied when trying to run: %s\n", claudeCmd)
137			errorMsg.WriteString("\n")
138			errorMsg.WriteString("  💡 Here's how to fix this:\n")
139			fmt.Fprintf(&errorMsg, "     • Check file permissions: chmod +x %s\n", claudeCmd)
140			errorMsg.WriteString("     • Try running with elevated permissions if needed")
141		case "unexpected_output":
142			fmt.Fprintf(&errorMsg, "  ⚠️  Unexpected output from 'claude -v': %s\n", strings.TrimSpace(stderrOutput))
143			errorMsg.WriteString("\n")
144			errorMsg.WriteString("  💡 This might not be Claude Code. Expected output containing '(Claude Code)'.\n")
145			errorMsg.WriteString("     • Make sure you have Claude Code installed, not a different 'claude' command\n")
146			errorMsg.WriteString("     • Visit https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/quickstart for installation")
147		default:
148			errorMsg.WriteString("  ⚠️  Error running 'claude -v'\n")
149			if stderrOutput != "" {
150				fmt.Fprintf(&errorMsg, "  📋 Error details: %s\n", stderrOutput)
151			}
152			errorMsg.WriteString("\n")
153			errorMsg.WriteString("  💡 Troubleshooting tips:\n")
154			errorMsg.WriteString("     • Make sure Claude Code is properly installed\n")
155			errorMsg.WriteString("     • Try running 'claude -v' directly in your terminal\n")
156			errorMsg.WriteString("     • Check https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code/quickstart for installation help")
157		}
158	
159		return errorMsg.String()
160	}
161	
162	// Check verifies Claude Code installation and returns version info
163	func (p *Provider) Check(customCommand string) spi.CheckResult {
164		// Parse the command (no resume session for version check)
165		claudeCmd, _ := parseClaudeCommand(customCommand, "")
166	
167		// Determine if custom command was used
168		isCustomCommand := customCommand != ""
169	
170		// Resolve the actual path of the command
171		resolvedPath := claudeCmd
172		if !filepath.IsAbs(claudeCmd) {
173			// Try to find the command in PATH
174			if path, err := exec.LookPath(claudeCmd); err == nil {
175				resolvedPath = path
176			}
177		}
178	
179		// Run claude -v to check version (ignore custom args for version check)
180		cmd := exec.Command(claudeCmd, "-v")
181		var out bytes.Buffer
182		var errOut bytes.Buffer
183		cmd.Stdout = &out
184		cmd.Stderr = &errOut
185	
186		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
187			// Track installation check failure
188			errorType := "unknown"
189	
190			var execErr *exec.Error
191			var pathErr *os.PathError
192	
193			// Check error types in order of specificity
194			switch {
195			case errors.As(err, &execErr) && execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound:
196				errorType = "not_found"
197			case errors.As(err, &pathErr):
198				if errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrNotExist) {
199					errorType = "not_found"
200				} else if errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrPermission) {
201					errorType = "permission_denied"
202				}
203			case errors.Is(err, os.ErrPermission):
204				errorType = "permission_denied"
205			}
206	
207			stderrOutput := strings.TrimSpace(errOut.String())
208			analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, analytics.Properties{
209				"provider":       "claude",
210				"custom_command": isCustomCommand,
211				"command_path":   claudeCmd,
212				"error_type":     errorType,
213				"error_message":  err.Error(),
214			})
215	
216			errorMessage := buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType, claudeCmd, isCustomCommand, stderrOutput)
217	
218			return spi.CheckResult{
219				Success:      false,
220				Version:      "",
221				Location:     resolvedPath,
222				ErrorMessage: errorMessage,
223			}
224		}
225	
226		// Check if output contains "Claude Code"
227		output := out.String()
228		if !strings.Contains(output, "(Claude Code)") {
229			// Track unexpected output error
230			analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, analytics.Properties{
231				"provider":       "claude",
232				"custom_command": isCustomCommand,
233				"command_path":   claudeCmd,
234				"error_type":     "unexpected_output",
235				"output":         strings.TrimSpace(output),
236			})
237	
238			errorMessage := buildCheckErrorMessage("unexpected_output", claudeCmd, isCustomCommand, output)
239	
240			return spi.CheckResult{
241				Success:      false,
242				Version:      "",
243				Location:     resolvedPath,
244				ErrorMessage: errorMessage,
245			}
246		}
247	
248		// Success! Track it
249		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallSuccess, analytics.Properties{
250			"provider":       "claude",
251			"custom_command": isCustomCommand,
252			"command_path":   resolvedPath,
253			"version":        strings.TrimSpace(output),
254		})
255	
256		return spi.CheckResult{
257			Success:      true,
258			Version:      strings.TrimSpace(output),
259			Location:     resolvedPath,
260			ErrorMessage: "",
261		}
262	}

264	// DetectAgent checks if Claude Code has been used in the given project directory
265	func (p *Provider) DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool {
266		// Get the Claude Code project directory for the given path
267		claudeProjectPath, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
268		if err != nil {
269			slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Failed to get Claude Code project directory", "error", err)
270			return false
271		}
272	
273		// Check if the Claude Code project directory exists
274		if info, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectPath); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
275			slog.Debug("DetectAgent: Claude Code project found", "path", claudeProjectPath)
276			return true
277		}
278	
279		slog.Debug("DetectAgent: No Claude Code project found", "expected_path", claudeProjectPath)
280	
281		// If helpOutput is requested, provide helpful guidance
282		if helpOutput {
283			fmt.Println() // Add visual separation
284			log.UserWarn("No Claude Code project found for this directory.\n")
285			log.UserMessage("Claude Code hasn't created a project folder for your current directory yet.\n")
286			log.UserMessage("This happens when Claude Code hasn't been run in this directory.\n\n")
287			log.UserMessage("To fix this:\n")
288			log.UserMessage("  1. Run 'specstory run' to start Claude Code in this directory\n")
289			log.UserMessage("  2. Or run Claude Code directly with `claude`, then try syncing again\n\n")
290			log.UserMessage("Expected project folder: %s\n", claudeProjectPath)
291			fmt.Println() // Add trailing newline
292		}
293	
294		return false
295	}

297	// GetAgentChatSessions retrieves all chat sessions for the given project path
298	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
299		// Get the Claude Code project directory
300		claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
301		if err != nil {
302			return nil, err
303		}
304	
305		// Check if project directory exists
306		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
307			return []spi.AgentChatSession{}, nil // No sessions if no project
308		}
309	
310		// Parse JSONL files with progress callback
311		parser := NewJSONLParser()
312		err = parser.ParseProjectSessionsWithProgress(claudeProjectDir, progress)
313		if err != nil {
314			return nil, err
315		}
316	
317		// Convert sessions to AgentChatSession structs
318		result := make([]spi.AgentChatSession, 0, len(parser.Sessions))
319		for _, session := range parser.Sessions {
320			// Skip empty sessions (no records)
321			if len(session.Records) == 0 {
322				continue
323			}
324	
325			// Process session (filter warmup, generate SessionData)
326			chatSession := processSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
327			if chatSession != nil {
328				result = append(result, *chatSession)
329			}
330		}
331	
332		return result, nil
333	}
334	
335	// GetAgentChatSession retrieves a single chat session by ID for the given project path
336	func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSession(projectPath string, sessionID string, debugRaw bool) (*spi.AgentChatSession, error) {
337		// Get the Claude Code project directory
338		claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
339		if err != nil {
340			return nil, err
341		}
342	
343		// Check if project directory exists
344		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
345			return nil, nil // No sessions if no project
346		}
347	
348		// Parse only the session we're looking for
349		parser := NewJSONLParser()
350		err = parser.ParseSingleSession(claudeProjectDir, sessionID)
351		if err != nil {
352			// If session not found, return nil (not an error)
353			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "not found") {
354				return nil, nil
355			}
356			return nil, err
357		}
358	
359		// Find the session
360		sessionPointer := parser.FindSession(sessionID)
361		if sessionPointer == nil {
362			return nil, nil
363		}
364		session := *sessionPointer
365	
366		// Skip empty sessions (no records)
367		if len(session.Records) == 0 {
368			return nil, nil
369		}
370	
371		// Process session (filter warmup, generate SessionData)
372		return processSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw), nil
373	}
```

```text
375	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes Claude Code in interactive mode and watches for session updates
376	func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
377		slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: Starting Claude Code execution and monitoring",
378			"projectPath", projectPath,
379			"customCommand", customCommand,
380			"resumeSessionID", resumeSessionID,
381			"debugRaw", debugRaw)
382	
383		// Validate resume session ID if provided (Claude Code uses UUID format)
384		if resumeSessionID != "" {
385			resumeSessionID = strings.TrimSpace(resumeSessionID)
386			// Simple UUID validation for Claude Code sessions
387			if len(resumeSessionID) != 36 || !strings.Contains(resumeSessionID, "-") {
388				return fmt.Errorf("invalid session ID format for Claude Code: %s (must be a UUID)", resumeSessionID)
389			}
390			slog.Info("Resuming Claude Code session", "sessionId", resumeSessionID)
391		}
392	
393		// Set up the callback which enables real-time markdown generation and cloud sync during
394		// interactive sessions. As Claude Code writes JSONL updates, the watcher detects
395		// changes and invokes this callback, allowing immediate processing without blocking
396		// the agent's execution. The defer ensures cleanup when the agent exits.
397		SetWatcherCallback(sessionCallback)
398		defer ClearWatcherCallback()
399	
400		// Set debug raw mode for the watcher
401		SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw)
402	
403		// Start watching for project directory in the background
404		slog.Info("Initializing project directory monitoring...")
405	
406		if err := WatchForProjectDir(); err != nil {
407			// Log the error but don't fail - watcher might work later
408			slog.Error("Failed to start project directory watcher", "error", err)
409		}
410	
411		// Execute Claude Code - this blocks until Claude exits
412		slog.Info("Executing Claude Code", "command", customCommand)
413		err := ExecuteClaude(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
414	
415		// Stop the watcher goroutine before returning
416		slog.Info("Claude Code has exited, stopping watcher")
417		StopWatcher()
418	
419		// Return any execution error
420		if err != nil {
421			return fmt.Errorf("claude Code execution failed: %w", err)
422		}
423	
424		return nil
425	}
426	
427	// WatchAgent watches for Claude Code agent activity and calls the callback with AgentChatSession
428	// Does NOT execute the agent - only watches for existing activity
429	// Runs until error or context cancellation
430	func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
431		slog.Info("WatchAgent: Starting Claude Code activity monitoring",
432			"projectPath", projectPath,
433			"debugRaw", debugRaw)
434	
435		// The watcher callback directly passes AgentChatSession (which now contains SessionData)
436		wrappedCallback := func(agentChatSession *spi.AgentChatSession) {
437			slog.Debug("WatchAgent: Received AgentChatSession",
438				"sessionID", agentChatSession.SessionID,
439				"hasSessionData", agentChatSession.SessionData != nil)
440	
441			// Call the user's callback with the AgentChatSession
442			sessionCallback(agentChatSession)
443		}
444	
445		// Set up the callback for the watcher
446		SetWatcherCallback(wrappedCallback)
447		defer ClearWatcherCallback()
448	
449		// Set debug raw mode for the watcher
450		SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw)
451	
452		// Get the Claude Code project directory for the specified project path
453		slog.Info("WatchAgent: Initializing project directory monitoring...")
454	
455		claudeProjectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(projectPath)
456		if err != nil {
457			slog.Error("WatchAgent: Failed to get Claude Code project directory", "error", err)
458			return fmt.Errorf("failed to get project directory: %w", err)
459		}
460	
461		slog.Info("WatchAgent: Project directory found", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
462	
463		if err := startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir); err != nil {
464			slog.Error("WatchAgent: Failed to start project watcher", "error", err)
465			return fmt.Errorf("failed to start watcher: %w", err)
466		}
467	
468		// Block until context is cancelled
469		// The watcher runs in a background goroutine and will continue
470		// until context is cancelled or the process exits
471		slog.Info("WatchAgent: Watcher started, blocking until context cancelled")
472	
473		// Wait for context cancellation
474		<-ctx.Done()
475	
476		slog.Info("WatchAgent: Context cancelled, stopping watcher")
477		StopWatcher()
478	
479		return ctx.Err()
480	}
481	
482	// isSyntheticMessage checks if a message is synthetic/internal and should be skipped
483	// when looking for the first real user message.
484	// Currently filters warmup messages and Claude Code session title generation prompts.
485	func isSyntheticMessage(content string) bool {
486		if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(content), "warmup") {
487			return true
488		}
489		// Claude Code wraps the real user prompt in <TEXTBLOCK> tags for title generation
490		if strings.Contains(content, "<TEXTBLOCK>") {
491			return true
492		}
493		return false
494	}
495	
496	// extractContentText extracts plain text from a message content field.
497	// The content field may be either a plain string or a JSON array of typed content
498	// blocks (e.g. [{type:"text",text:"..."},{type:"tool_use",...}]).
499	// When it's an array, all "text"-type blocks are joined with newlines.
500	// This handles the Claude Code JSONL format where IDE context tags (e.g.
501	// <ide_opened_file>) are injected as separate text blocks before the real question.
502	func extractContentText(content interface{}) string {
503		switch v := content.(type) {
504		case string:
505			return v
506		case []interface{}:
507			var parts []string
508			for _, block := range v {
509				if m, ok := block.(map[string]interface{}); ok {
510					if t, _ := m["type"].(string); t == "text" {
511						if text, ok := m["text"].(string); ok && text != "" {
512							parts = append(parts, text)
513						}
514					}
515				}
516			}
517			slog.Debug("extractContentText: extracted text from content block array",
518				"blockCount", len(v),
519				"textParts", len(parts))
520			return strings.Join(parts, "\n")
521		default:
522			return ""
523		}
524	}
```

# Code Review — PR #218: Add DeepSeek TUI provider

I've used `pkg/providers/claudecode/` as the canonical reference and called out divergences from that pattern.

---

## `pkg/config/config.go`

**Lines 113–116 (added):** New comment block + commented-out `# deepseek_cmd = "deepseek"` in `defaultConfigTemplate`.
- (1) ✅ Consistent with the existing pattern for other providers in the same template.

**Lines 192–198 (modified):** `ProvidersConfig` struct gains a `DeepseekCmd` field, alphabetically sorted.
- (2) ✅ Alphabetical insertion preserves the existing convention.
- (3) ⚠️ Tag is `toml:"deepseek_cmd"` and Go field is `DeepseekCmd` — but the brand uses "DeepSeek" with internal capital S. The other Go field `CodexCmd` is fine because "Codex" is one word; for "DeepSeek" the more idiomatic Go would be `DeepSeekCmd`. Minor consistency call.

**Lines 736–738 (added):** `GetProviderCmd` adds a `"deepseek"` case.
- (4) ✅ Wired into the right switch in the right alphabetical slot.

---

## `pkg/providers/deepseek/agent_session.go`

**Lines 62–71:** Schema type aliases.
- (5) ✅ Same pattern as `droidcli` and `geminicli` — good consistency.

**Lines 73–110:** `dsSession` / `dsMetadata` / `dsMessage` / `dsContentPart` types modeling the on-disk JSON.
- (6) ✅ Comment "// populated after parse for debugRaw output" explains the WHY for `RawData string \`json:"-"\``.
- (7) ⚠️ `dsContentPart` is a "fat" union struct — it carries fields for every content `Type` (text/thinking/tool_use/tool_result). Idiomatic Go alternative is a typed dispatch via `json.RawMessage` + a switch, but the fat-struct approach is fine here and matches `droidcli` precedent.
- (8) ⚠️ `Input map[string]interface{}` — codebase prefers `map[string]any` in newer files (mixed in this PR — both `interface{}` and `any` appear; see `markdown_tools.go`). Pick one.

**Lines 112–126 — `parseSessionFile`:**
- (9) ✅ Clean: read → unmarshal → set RawData → return.
- (10) ⚠️ `RawData` always stores the full file even when `debugRaw` is off. For long sessions this allocates the whole JSON body into memory just to be discarded by every caller except `writeDebugRaw`. Consider passing `debugRaw` down so RawData is only populated when needed.

**Lines 128–169 — `extractSessionMetadata`:**
- (11) ⚠️ Comment says "without parsing the full messages array" but the code immediately calls `parseSessionFile` which DOES parse everything. The comment is now misleading.
- (12) ⚠️ Returns `nil, nil` twice for "no messages" / "no user text" — `nil, nil` is unusual; calling code in `ListAgentChatSessions` handles it, but a `(*spi.SessionMetadata, error)` returning both nil is a smell. An explicit sentinel or an empty result would be clearer.
- (13) ⚠️ Multiple returns — not strictly single-exit per CLAUDE.md. The guard clauses are OK (immediate guards are allowed), but `extractSessionMetadata` has three returns that could be consolidated to one.

**Lines 171–204 — `convertToAgentSession`:**
- (14) ✅ Two guard clauses up top, single return — good.
- (15) ✅ Defaults `slug` if empty — defensive.

**Lines 206–244 — `generateAgentSession`:**
- (16) ✅ Excellent WHY comment at lines 224–226 explaining why `Usage` is intentionally not synthesized. This is exactly the project's "why" comment style.
- (17) ✅ Workspace override logic (caller→metadata) is straightforward.

**Lines 246–315 — `buildExchanges`:**
- (18) ✅ Top-of-function WHY comment (lines 254–257) explaining the deterministic timestamp choice — good comment, test confirms it (`TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps`).
- (19) ✅ The exchange-grouping rule (tool_result-only user messages don't open a new exchange) is explained in the comment on lines 247–249.
- (20) ⚠️ The function mutates the loop variable: `attachToolResults(&msg, current)` and `convertUserMessage(msg, ...)` — `msg` is a copy from `range`. Passing `&msg` is intentional but a Go gotcha worth a one-liner comment if kept.
- (21) ⚠️ Mixed responsibility — this function does both grouping AND ID/timestamp backfill (the trailing loop at 304–312). Could be cleaner split, but acceptable.

**Lines 317–334 — `isToolResultOnly`:**
- (22) ✅ Clear name, well-commented intent, well-tested (`TestIsToolResultOnly`).

**Lines 336–384 — `attachToolResults`:**
- (23) ✅ Excellent WHY comment (lines 338–348) — describes the bug being fixed and why the index-by-UseID approach is correct. This is the gold standard for project comments.
- (24) ✅ Test coverage for this is strong (`TestAttachToolResults_ParallelToolCalls` — explicitly tests the bug being fixed).
- (25) ✅ Re-renders `FormattedMarkdown` once the result is attached — comment at 378 explains why.

**Lines 386–412 — `convertUserMessage` / `userContentParts`:**
- (26) ✅ `<turn_meta>` filtering is documented at 387–388. Good.
- (27) ⚠️ Two passes over content (once in `convertUserMessage` → `userContentParts`, once elsewhere in `msgContentText`) — minor duplication.

**Lines 414–459 — `convertAssistantMessage`:**
- (28) ⚠️ Lines 428–429 and 436–438 have an odd fallback: text content falls back to thinking, and thinking falls back to text. These cross-fallbacks would only fire if a content part has the wrong `Type` for its data. Comment-worthy if intentional, deletable if not.
- (29) ✅ "each tool_use becomes its own message" is tested.

**Lines 461–485 — `convertToolUseMessage`:**
- (30) ✅ Good comment about the two-phase render at 462–463.
- (31) ⚠️ Hardcoded fallback `name = "unknown"` — fine, but no log. If we're seeing nameless tool_uses it's probably a parser bug; `slog.Debug` would help.

**Lines 487–525 — `classifyToolType`:**
- (32) ⚠️ Five `map[string]bool` literals built on EVERY call. Move to package-level `var` once. This will be called once per assistant tool message — not perf-critical, but the idiom is to declare these once.
- (33) ⚠️ Multiple returns (6) instead of single exit. Could be a single map lookup or a switch.

**Lines 527–548 — `msgContentText`:**
- (34) ⚠️ Two-pass loop (skip turn_meta first, then fallback to thinking) is OK but could be a single pass.

**Lines 550–562 — `deriveSlug`:**
- (35) ✅ Simple and clear.

**Lines 564–583 — `writeDebugRaw`:**
- (36) ✅ Uses `slog.Debug` consistently.
- (37) ✅ Permissions `0o755` / `0o644` are correct.

---

## `pkg/providers/deepseek/deepseek_exec.go`

**Lines 1130–1141 — `expandTilde`:**
- (38) ✅ Simple, scoped to leading `~/`, has fallback for `os.UserHomeDir()` failure.

**Lines 1143–1153 — `parseCommand`:**
- (39) ✅ Reuses `spi.SplitCommandLine` — DRY.

**Lines 1155–1169 — `ExecuteDeepSeek`:**
- (40) ⚠️ Only one `slog.Info` at the resume case. Compare to `claudecode.ExecuteClaude` which logs the command line, environment, etc. — at minimum, log "Executing DeepSeek TUI" with command. The CLAUDE.md guidance is "Provide consistent observability and tracing with log/slog."
- (41) ⚠️ No analytics tracking around launch. `claudecode` doesn't track here either, so this is consistent — flag for awareness only.

**Lines 1171–1199 — `ensureResumeArgs`:**
- (42) ✅ Handles `--resume`, `-r`, `--resume=value`, dangling forms — well-tested in `TestEnsureResumeArgs`.
- (43) ⚠️ The `repaired := append([]string{}, args[:i+1]...); repaired = append(repaired, ...)` pattern is correct (avoids aliasing into the caller's slice — verified by `TestParseCommand_DoesNotMutate`) but a one-line comment explaining why we copy would help.

---

## `pkg/providers/deepseek/markdown_tools.go`

**Lines 1444–1465 — `formatToolCall`:**
- (44) ✅ Good WHY comment (1444–1449) about the two-phase render.

**Lines 1467–1498 — `formatToolInput`:**
- (45) ⚠️ Long switch on normalized name. The two `default` branches at 1493–1494 and 1495–1496 both call `renderGenericJSON(args)` — the second case (`taskcreate/taskread/tasklist/note`) is unreachable as it falls through identically to default. Drop or fix it.

**Lines 1500–1513 — `formatToolOutput`:**
- (46) ✅ Clean.

**Lines 1515–1565 — `extractPathHints`:**
- (47) ❌ **Significant DRY duplication** with `droidcli/agent_session.go:350`. The comment at 1517–1518 explicitly acknowledges it: "Mirrors droidcli's extractPathHints — same shell-command extraction." The path-field list and shell-command extraction logic are nearly identical. Per CLAUDE.md: "Emphasize DRY code, look for existing code that can be reused." This should live in `spi/` (like `spi.ExtractShellPathHints` already does) and be reused. Recommend lifting into `spi`.

**Lines 1567–1579 — `addPathHint`:**
- (48) ❌ **Verbatim copy** of `droidcli.addPathHint`. Same DRY concern — move to `spi/`.

**Lines 1583–1733 — Per-tool input renderers (renderRead, renderList, renderGrep, renderFileSearch, renderWebSearch, renderWebFetch, renderWrite, renderEdit, renderApplyPatch, renderTodoWrite, formatExecuteInput, formatDiffBlock, formatContentBlock, languageFromPath):**
- (49) ⚠️ Most of these mirror functions in `droidcli/markdown_tools.go` very closely. Same DRY concern.

**Lines 1751–1772 — `formatDiffBlock`:**
- (50) ⚠️ `for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n")` + an `if oldText == "" { break }` inside the loop is awkward — Go's `strings.Split("", "\n")` yields `[""]` so the loop runs once with an empty line. The early-break works but is confusing; idiomatic would be `if oldText != "" { for _, line := range strings.Split(oldText, "\n") { ... } }`.

**Lines 1803–1825 — `renderGenericJSON`:**
- (51) ❌ Verbatim duplicate of `droidcli.renderGenericJSON`.

**Lines 1827–1841 — `renderJSONValue`:**
- (52) ❌ Near-duplicate of `droidcli.renderJSONValue` (slightly fewer cases — e.g. missing `[]byte`). Drift risk.

**Lines 1843–1865 — `stringValue`:**
- (53) ❌ Near-duplicate of `droidcli.stringValue`. The droid version handles more numeric types (`float32`, `int32`, `uint`, `uint64`, `fmt.Stringer`). The drift means callers in deepseek will silently miss fields containing those types.

**Lines 1867–1873 — `normalizeToolName`:**
- (54) ❌ Verbatim duplicate of `droidcli.normalizeToolName`.

**Lines 1875–1884 — `todoSymbol`:**
- (55) ⚠️ The `"⚡"` symbol inside a markdown checkbox `- [⚡]` is unusual — most markdown renderers expect a single character (space/`x`). May render oddly. Compare droidcli's behavior.

---

## `pkg/providers/deepseek/path_utils.go`

**Lines 2258–2261 — Constants:**
- (56) ✅ Cleanly named.

**Lines 2269–2276 — `resolveSessionsDir`:**
- (57) ✅ Single-exit with errors wrapped.

**Lines 2278–2321 — `listSessionFiles`:**
- (58) ✅ Skips dirs and non-`.json` entries, returns `[]sessionFile{}` (not nil) on ENOENT — good for callers.
- (59) ⚠️ The `info, err := entry.Info(); if err != nil { continue }` swallows errors silently — at least `slog.Debug` it.

**Lines 2323–2338 — `findSessionFileByID`:**
- (60) ⚠️ Returns `("", nil)` for not-found. The empty-string-not-error pattern is OK but inconsistent with Go conventions; an explicit `os.ErrNotExist` would be clearer. Caller in `provider.go:2802` checks `path == ""`, so this works.
- (61) ⚠️ Calls `listSessionFiles` (full dir scan + sort) when you only need to check `<sessionID>.json` existence. A direct `os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, sessionID+".json"))` would be O(1) vs O(N).

**Lines 2340–2346 — `trimExt`:**
- (62) ⚠️ This is `strings.TrimSuffix(name, filepath.Ext(name))` in one line. The custom helper isn't needed.

**Lines 2348–2367 — `sessionMentionsProject`:**
- (63) ✅ Good — uses the fast extractor on the hot path.

**Lines 2369–2401 — `extractWorkspaceFast`:**
- (64) ✅ Excellent WHY comment (2370–2372) explaining why streaming matters.
- (65) ⚠️ This is a textual JSON parser — fragile. If DeepSeek ever pretty-prints with `"workspace": "value"` (extra spaces inside the key) or embeds a `\u0022` escape in metadata, this breaks. Tests cover the happy paths but not edge cases like escaped quotes inside the workspace value. Worth a comment acknowledging the trade-off.
- (66) ⚠️ Returns "" for both "missing file" and "no workspace key" — caller can't distinguish. The fact that the function silently swallows `os.Open` errors (line 2376) is not logged.

**Lines 2403–2424 — `matchWorkspace`:**
- (67) ⚠️ Hand-rolled JSON string parser doesn't handle escape sequences (`\"`, `\\`, `\n` etc.) in the workspace value. Unlikely for filesystem paths but worth a comment.

**Lines 2426–2440 — `canonicalizePath`:**
- (68) ✅ Three-tier fallback (canonical → abs → clean) is reasonable.

---

## `pkg/providers/deepseek/provider.go`

**Lines 2679–2682 — Constants:**
- (69) ✅ Good — `defaultCommand` and `versionFlag` shared between exec and check.

**Lines 2684–2727 — `Check`:**
- (70) ⚠️ The classification logic (`classifyCheckError`) does NOT recognize ENOENT errors from `os.PathError` — `claudecode.Check` does. If `exec.LookPath` succeeds but the file is later removed, this would mis-classify as `version_failed`. Minor edge case.
- (71) ⚠️ Error messages are simple text. Compare `claudecode.buildCheckErrorMessage`, which uses emoji, numbered steps, and a docs link — significantly more user-friendly. The team might want consistency with the canonical provider here.
- (72) ✅ Analytics tracking on both success and failure paths — uses the same event names as other providers.

**Lines 2729–2757 — `DetectAgent`:**
- (73) ✅ Empty projectPath returns true if any sessions exist (matches gemini/droid convention; tested).
- (74) ⚠️ The full-scan `for _, file := range files { if sessionMentionsProject(...) }` walks every session on disk to detect one match. For users with many sessions this can be slow. Compare `claudecode` which checks for project dir existence — O(1). Acceptable for now but log-worthy.
- (75) ⚠️ Multiple returns — could consolidate to a single result variable.

**Lines 2759–2798 — `GetAgentChatSessions`:**
- (76) ✅ Pre-filters by project before parsing — saves unnecessary JSON unmarshaling.
- (77) ⚠️ `progress` is called on errors too (line 2785) — that's actually correct (progress should advance even on skip), but droidcli doesn't do this. Minor consistency note.

**Lines 2800–2814 — `GetAgentChatSession`:**
- (78) ⚠️ Two return-nil-nil cases (`path == ""` and project mismatch) — both silent. A `slog.Debug` would help diagnose "why didn't my session sync."

**Lines 2816–2842 — `ExecAgentAndWatch`:**
- (79) ⚠️ Sparse logging vs claudecode which logs at every lifecycle stage (start, watch init, command, exit, watcher stopped). Adding a few `slog.Info` calls would match the project convention from CLAUDE.md.
- (80) ✅ Goroutine + context cancel for the watcher is sound; non-nil watcher errors are logged unless `context.Canceled`.

**Lines 2844–2851 — `WatchAgent`:**
- (81) ⚠️ Returns `fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")` but `watchSessions` (line 3141) does the same check. Redundant.

**Lines 2853–2881 — `ListAgentChatSessions`:**
- (82) ✅ Clean: filter → extract metadata → collect.

**Lines 2885–2898 — `classifyCheckError`:**
- (83) ❌ **DRY:** Same logic exists in `droidcli/provider.go:230`. Should be a shared helper in `spi/`.

**Lines 2900–2925 — `buildCheckErrorMessage`:**
- (84) ⚠️ Similar in structure to `droidcli.buildCheckErrorMessage` and `claudecode.buildCheckErrorMessage` but each provider has its own messages. The shape (errorType switch + Fprintf) could be templated.

**Lines 2927–2953 — `trackCheckSuccess` / `trackCheckFailure`:**
- (85) ❌ **DRY:** Verbatim duplicates of `droidcli`'s helpers (just `"deepseek"` substituted). Should live in `spi/` or `analytics/`.

**Lines 2955–2958 — `printDetectionHelp`:**
- (86) ⚠️ Hardcoded path `~/.deepseek/sessions` in the help text — fine, but the constants from `path_utils.go` could be used.

---

## `pkg/providers/deepseek/watcher.go`

**Lines 3120–3131 — Imports:**
- (87) ⚠️ `"strings"` is in its own import group (line 3128). Run `gofmt -w` / `goimports` — should be in the same group as `"os"`, `"path/filepath"`.

**Lines 3134–3136 — `watchState`:**
- (88) ⚠️ The struct only has `lastProcessed map[string]int64`. A bare `map` would suffice; the struct adds no semantic value yet. Keep if you anticipate adding state.

**Lines 3138–3187 — `watchSessions`:**
- (89) ✅ Mirrors `droidcli.watchSessions` — good structural consistency.
- (90) ⚠️ No mutex around `state.lastProcessed`. `handleWatchEvent` writes from one goroutine, but `dispatchSession` (line 3309) spawns a goroutine for the callback — if any caller mutates the session map via callbacks, this races. Currently safe because all map access happens on the single event-loop goroutine; but worth a comment ("only mutated on the event loop").

**Lines 3189–3207 — `setupWatcher`:**
- (91) ✅ Walks up the tree if `~/.deepseek/sessions` doesn't exist yet — sensible for new installs.

**Lines 3209–3246 — `handleWatchEvent`:**
- (92) ⚠️ Multiple returns (6+ exit points). Could be flattened with early-return guards already present, but readable.

**Lines 3248–3277 — `processSessionFile`:**
- (93) ✅ Modtime-based deduplication is correct.

**Lines 3279–3306 — `scanAndProcessSessions`:**
- (94) ⚠️ Very similar body to `processSessionFile` — duplicate parse/convert/dispatch logic. Extract a helper.

**Lines 3308–3321 — `dispatchSession`:**
- (95) ✅ Panic recovery wrapper is good defensive practice.
- (96) ⚠️ Spawning a goroutine per session callback may cause unbounded concurrency on a burst of events. Compare claudecode's synchronous callback path. Consider a bounded worker pool, or just call synchronously.

---

## `pkg/spi/factory/registry.go`

**Lines 3326–3340 — Provider registration:**
- (97) ✅ Import added in correct alphabetical position; registration block matches the existing pattern.

---

# Tests review

The PR adds 5 test files (~1.25K lines). Most tests are **good** — well-targeted, data-driven, and verify the non-trivial parts of the conversion logic.

**Strong tests (catch real bugs, non-trivial):**
- `TestAttachToolResults_ParallelToolCalls` — directly tests the bug described in the comment. Excellent.
- `TestBuildExchanges_DeterministicTimestamps` — protects against the `time.Now()` regression.
- `TestBuildExchanges_FallbackToCreatedAt` — covers the fallback path.
- `TestIsToolResultOnly` — data-driven, covers `<turn_meta>` filtering edge cases.
- `TestConvertAssistantMessage` — data-driven, covers the tool/text/thinking partitioning.
- `TestExtractWorkspaceFast` — covers the textual-extraction edge case where workspace appears after messages.
- `TestExtractWorkspaceFast_LargeLine` — confirms the 1MB buffer is wired up.
- `TestExtractPathHints` — covers shell command extraction + de-dup + array values.

**Weak / tautological tests (consider removing):**
- (T1) `TestVersionFlag` in `provider_test.go` (lines 2974–2978): asserts `versionFlag == "--version"`. Tests Go literal, not logic.
- (T2) `TestProviderName` (2980–2985): asserts `Name() == "DeepSeek TUI"`. Same — tests a hardcoded return.
- (T3) `TestDefaultCommandConstant` in `deepseek_exec_test.go` (1396–1404): asserts `defaultCommand == "deepseek"` and `versionFlag == "--version"`. Same as above. Comment says "user contract" but a constant test doesn't actually defend a contract.
- (T4) `TestExpandTilde` (1369–1394): four cases that exercise trivial string prefix logic. Marginal.
- (T5) `TestParseCommand_DoesNotMutate` (1406–1424): the "guard against accidental in-place modification" is testing Go semantics for slice copying. Borderline.
- (T6) `TestClassifyToolType` (1001–1023): straightforward map lookup. Useful only if you expect tool-name lists to drift — borderline.
- (T7) `TestConvertAssistantMessage_AttachesPathHints` (977–999): verifies `extractPathHints` is called from the right place — fine, but mostly verifies wiring rather than logic.

**Per CLAUDE.md "Better to not have a unit test than to have tests that are simplistic or tautological"** — T1, T2, T3 (and arguably T4) should be deleted.

---

# Top 5 most important improvements

1. **DRY up duplicated helpers with droidcli** (observations 47, 48, 51–54, 83, 85). At minimum: `classifyCheckError`, `trackCheckSuccess/Failure`, `renderGenericJSON`, `stringValue`, `normalizeToolName`, `extractPathHints`, `addPathHint`. Move to `pkg/spi/` (or `pkg/spi/markdown/`). The drift in `stringValue` (deepseek's misses several numeric types vs. droidcli) is a concrete bug-class waiting to happen.

2. **Add lifecycle slog.Info logging in `ExecAgentAndWatch` and `Check`** (40, 79). CLAUDE.md explicitly calls for "consistent observability." Compare to claudecode's logging density.

3. **Delete tautological tests (T1, T2, T3, possibly T4)** — they assert literal constants and dilute the signal of the rest of the suite.

4. **Misleading comment in `extractSessionMetadata`** (11): the comment claims it avoids parsing the full messages array, but the implementation does the opposite. Either fix the comment or refactor to actually stream-parse just the metadata block (the `extractWorkspaceFast` infrastructure is already there).

5. **Reachability bug in `formatToolInput`** (45): the `case "taskcreate", "taskread", "tasklist", "note":` branch returns the same thing as `default:`. Dead code — drop it, or differentiate the rendering.

# Optional / nice-to-haves

- **(61)** Avoid full directory scan in `findSessionFileByID` — direct `os.Stat` of `<sessionID>.json` is O(1).
- **(96)** Consider whether `dispatchSession` should spawn a goroutine per event or call synchronously like claudecode does.
- **(62)** Replace custom `trimExt` with `strings.TrimSuffix(name, filepath.Ext(name))`.
- **(87)** Run `gofmt`/`goimports` on `watcher.go` — imports are split into two groups.

# Test gaps worth filling

- `extractWorkspaceFast` doesn't cover escaped quotes inside the workspace value (`"workspace": "/path/with \"quote\""`) — the textual parser will mis-extract. Worth either testing the limitation or making the parser robust.
- No test for `ExecAgentAndWatch`'s context cancellation path — only the no-callback shortcut is exercised.
- No test confirming `dispatchSession`'s panic recovery actually keeps the watcher alive after a panicking callback.

```text
18		ProviderInfo = schema.ProviderInfo
19		Exchange     = schema.Exchange
20		Message      = schema.Message
21		ContentPart  = schema.ContentPart
22		ToolInfo     = schema.ToolInfo
23		Usage        = schema.Usage
24	)

26	// dsSession represents the on-disk format of a DeepSeek TUI session file.
27	type dsSession struct {
28		SchemaVersion int         `json:"schema_version"`
29		Metadata      dsMetadata  `json:"metadata"`
30		Messages      []dsMessage `json:"messages"`
31		SystemPrompt  string      `json:"system_prompt"`
32		RawData       string      `json:"-"` // populated after parse for debugRaw output
33	}
34	
35	type dsMetadata struct {
36		ID           string `json:"id"`
37		Title        string `json:"title"`
38		CreatedAt    string `json:"created_at"`
39		UpdatedAt    string `json:"updated_at"`
40		MessageCount int    `json:"message_count"`
41		TotalTokens  int    `json:"total_tokens"`
42		Model        string `json:"model"`
43		Workspace    string `json:"workspace"`
44		Mode         string `json:"mode"`
45	}
46	
47	type dsMessage struct {
48		Role    string          `json:"role"`
49		Content []dsContentPart `json:"content"`
50	}
51	
52	type dsContentPart struct {
53		Type     string `json:"type"`     // "text", "thinking", "tool_use", "tool_result"
54		Text     string `json:"text"`     // used when Type == "text"
55		Thinking string `json:"thinking"` // used when Type == "thinking"
56		// tool_use fields are flat in the JSON (not nested)
57		Name  string                 `json:"name"`
58		ID    string                 `json:"id"`
59		Input map[string]interface{} `json:"input"`
60		// tool_result fields
61		ToolResultContent string `json:"content"`     // tool_result content (raw string)
62		ToolUseID         string `json:"tool_use_id"` // matches the tool_use.id this result is for
63	}
64	
65	// parseSessionFile reads and parses a DeepSeek TUI session JSON file.
66	func parseSessionFile(path string) (*dsSession, error) {
67		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
68		if err != nil {
69			return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot read session file %s: %w", path, err)
70		}
71	
72		var session dsSession
73		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &session); err != nil {
74			return nil, fmt.Errorf("deepseek: cannot parse session file %s: %w", path, err)
75		}
76	
77		session.RawData = string(data)
78		return &session, nil
79	}
80	
81	// extractSessionMetadata reads only the metadata fields from a session file
82	// without parsing the full messages array.
83	func extractSessionMetadata(path string) (*spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
84		// Parse full file since we need to find the first user message for name/slug.
85		session, err := parseSessionFile(path)
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, err
88		}
89	
90		if len(session.Messages) == 0 {
91			return nil, nil
92		}
93	
94		// Find first user message for slug and name
95		var firstUserMsg string
96		for _, msg := range session.Messages {
97			if msg.Role == "user" {
98				firstUserMsg = msgContentText(msg)
99				if firstUserMsg != "" {
100					break
101				}
102			}
103		}
104	
105		if firstUserMsg == "" {
106			return nil, nil
107		}
108	
109		slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(firstUserMsg)
110		if slug == "" {
111			slug = "deepseek-session"
112		}
113	
114		name := spi.GenerateReadableName(firstUserMsg)
115	
116		return &spi.SessionMetadata{
117			SessionID: session.Metadata.ID,
118			CreatedAt: session.Metadata.CreatedAt,
119			Slug:      slug,
120			Name:      name,
121		}, nil
122	}
123	
124	// convertToAgentSession converts a parsed dsSession to the unified AgentChatSession format.
125	func convertToAgentSession(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
126		if session == nil {
127			return nil
128		}
129		if len(session.Messages) == 0 {
130			return nil
131		}
132	
133		sessionData, err := generateAgentSession(session, workspaceRoot)
134		if err != nil {
135			slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session due to conversion error",
136				"sessionId", session.Metadata.ID, "error", err)
137			return nil
138		}
139	
140		// Derive slug from first user message
141		slug := deriveSlug(session)
142		if slug == "" {
143			slug = "deepseek-session"
144		}
145	
146		if debugRaw {
147			writeDebugRaw(session)
148		}
149	
150		return &spi.AgentChatSession{
151			SessionID:   session.Metadata.ID,
152			CreatedAt:   sessionData.CreatedAt,
153			Slug:        slug,
154			SessionData: sessionData,
155			RawData:     session.RawData,
156		}
157	}
158	
159	// generateAgentSession converts a dsSession into the shared SessionData schema.
160	func generateAgentSession(session *dsSession, workspaceRoot string) (*SessionData, error) {
161		workspace := strings.TrimSpace(workspaceRoot)
162		if ws := strings.TrimSpace(session.Metadata.Workspace); ws != "" {
163			workspace = ws
164		}
165	
166		exchanges := buildExchanges(session, workspace)
167		if len(exchanges) == 0 {
168			return nil, fmt.Errorf("session contains no conversational exchanges")
169		}
170
```

```text
161	// scanAndProcessSessions scans all session files and processes any that have been modified.
162	func scanAndProcessSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession), state *watchState) error {
163		files, err := listSessionFiles()
164		if err != nil {
165			return err
166		}
167	
168		for _, file := range files {
169			if last, ok := state.lastProcessed[file.Path]; ok && last >= file.ModTime {
170				continue
171			}
172			if projectPath != "" && !sessionMentionsProject(file.Path, projectPath) {
173				continue
174			}
175			session, err := parseSessionFile(file.Path)
176			if err != nil {
177				slog.Debug("deepseek: skipping session", "path", file.Path, "error", err)
178				continue
179			}
180			chat := convertToAgentSession(session, projectPath, debugRaw)
181			if chat == nil {
182				continue
183			}
184			state.lastProcessed[file.Path] = file.ModTime
```

All five fixes are in and the package builds, lints clean, and tests pass.

## Summary of changes

**Fix (3) — `pkg/config/config.go`**
Renamed field `DeepseekCmd` → `DeepSeekCmd` (matches the brand's internal capital S). TOML tag `deepseek_cmd` unchanged so user configs keep working.

**Fix (8) — `pkg/providers/deepseek/{agent_session,markdown_tools}.go`**
Normalized 6 spots in non-test source from `map[string]interface{}` / `[]interface{}` to `map[string]any` / `[]any` — the package's dominant form.

**Fix (10) — `pkg/providers/deepseek/agent_session.go`**
`parseSessionFile` now takes `wantRawData bool`. Metadata-only callers pass `false` (no 2× allocation of the JSON body); cloud-sync paths in `provider.go` and `watcher.go` pass `true` to keep `AgentChatSession.RawData` populated for `cloud.SyncSessionToCloud`.

**Fix (11) — `pkg/providers/deepseek/agent_session.go`**
Reworded `extractSessionMetadata`'s doc comment — was a lie ("reads only the metadata fields without parsing the full messages array"). Now accurately describes what it does: parses the file and summarizes via the first user message.

**Fix (13) — `pkg/providers/deepseek/agent_session.go`**
Consolidated `extractSessionMetadata` to a single exit point via a `result` variable. Extracted `firstUserMessageText` helper so the function is now linear and easier to read.

**Verification:** `go build ./...` ✅, `go test ./pkg/providers/deepseek/ ./pkg/config/` ✅, `golangci-lint run` on changed packages ✅ (0 issues after `gofmt -w`).

```text
39		isCustom := strings.TrimSpace(customCommand) != ""
40

44			trackCheckFailure("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, "", versionFlag, "", "not_found", err.Error())
45			return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: "", ErrorMessage: msg}
46		}
47	
48		var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
49		cmd := exec.Command(resolved, versionFlag)
50		cmd.Stdout = &stdout
51		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
52		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
53			errorType := classifyCheckError(err)
54			stderrOutput := strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String())
55			msg := buildCheckErrorMessage(errorType, resolved, isCustom, stderrOutput)
56			trackCheckFailure("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, versionFlag, stderrOutput, errorType, err.Error())
57			return spi.CheckResult{Success: false, Location: resolved, ErrorMessage: msg}
58		}
59	
60		version := strings.TrimSpace(stdout.String())
61		if version == "" {
62			version = "unknown"
63		}
64		trackCheckSuccess("deepseek", isCustom, cmdName, resolved, version, versionFlag)
65		return spi.CheckResult{Success: true, Version: version, Location: resolved}
66	}
```

```text
155	// ExecAgentAndWatch executes DeepSeek TUI for the given project and watches
156	// for session updates.
157	func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
158		if sessionCallback == nil {
159			return ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
160		}
161	
162		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
163		defer cancel()
164	
165		watchErr := make(chan error, 1)
166		go func() {
167			watchErr <- watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
168		}()
169	
170		err := ExecuteDeepSeek(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
171		cancel()
172	
173		if werr := <-watchErr; werr != nil && !errors.Is(werr, context.Canceled) {
174			slog.Warn("deepseek: watcher stopped with error", "error", werr)
175		}
176	
177		if err != nil {
178			return fmt.Errorf("deepseek execution failed: %w", err)
179		}
180		return nil
181	}
182	
183	// WatchAgent monitors DeepSeek TUI sessions for the given project and invokes
184	// sessionCallback for each new or updated session until ctx is canceled.

186		if sessionCallback == nil {
187			return fmt.Errorf("session callback is required")
188		}
189		return watchSessions(ctx, projectPath, debugRaw, sessionCallback)
190	}
```

```text
164	func TestExpandTilde(t *testing.T) {
165		home, err := os.UserHomeDir()
166		if err != nil {
167			t.Skip("UserHomeDir failed, skipping")
168		}
169	
170		tests := []struct {
171			name  string
172			input string
173			want  string
174		}{
175			{name: "no tilde returns input", input: "/usr/local/bin/foo", want: "/usr/local/bin/foo"},
176			{name: "bare tilde without slash returns input", input: "~tilde", want: "~tilde"},
177			{name: "tilde slash expands to home", input: "~/bin/foo", want: filepath.Join(home, "bin/foo")},
178			{name: "empty input returns empty", input: "", want: ""},
179		}
180	
181		for _, tt := range tests {
182			t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
183				got := expandTilde(tt.input)
184				if got != tt.want {
185					t.Errorf("expandTilde(%q) = %q, want %q", tt.input, got, tt.want)
186				}
187			})

191	func TestDefaultCommandConstant(t *testing.T) {
192		// Sanity: defaults shouldn't drift silently — they're part of the user contract.
193		if defaultCommand != "deepseek" {
194			t.Errorf("defaultCommand = %q, want %q", defaultCommand, "deepseek")
195		}

200	
201	func TestParseCommand_DoesNotMutate(t *testing.T) {
202		// Guard against accidental in-place modification of the input string,
203		// which would surprise callers that re-use the same custom-command value.
204		input := "deepseek --foo"
205		original := input
206		_, _ = parseCommand(input)
207		if input != original {
208			t.Errorf("parseCommand mutated input: got %q, want %q", input, original)
209		}
210		// Also verify args slice is independent (we can mutate without aliasing into spi internals).
211		_, args := parseCommand("deepseek --a --b")
212		if len(args) > 0 {
213			args[0] = "MUTATED"
214			_, args2 := parseCommand("deepseek --a --b")
215			if strings.Contains(strings.Join(args2, " "), "MUTATED") {
216				t.Errorf("parseCommand returned aliased slice; got %v", args2)
217			}
218		}
219	}
```

## Summary

**Fix (2) — Lifecycle logging:**
- `Check` (provider.go): added `slog.Info` at entry, after `LookPath` resolves, on version-probe failure (with errorType + stderr), and on success (with resolved + version).
- `ExecAgentAndWatch` (provider.go): added `slog.Info` at entry with all params, when running without callback, when launching the watcher goroutine, before/after executing DeepSeek, and on clean exit. Watcher-error log message prefixed with the function name for context.
- `WatchAgent` (provider.go): added entry log and exit log (previously had zero logging).

**Fix (3) — Tautological tests deleted:**
- `TestVersionFlag` (provider_test.go) — asserted `versionFlag == "--version"`
- `TestProviderName` (provider_test.go) — asserted hardcoded return value
- `TestDefaultCommandConstant` (deepseek_exec_test.go) — asserted both constants
- `TestExpandTilde` (deepseek_exec_test.go) — exercised trivial string-prefix logic that's effectively re-testing `strings.HasPrefix` + `filepath.Join`

**Fix (87) — watcher.go imports:** Merged the orphaned `"strings"` group into the stdlib block; `gofmt` now passes clean.

**Verification:** `go build ./...` ✅, lint clean (0 issues), all deepseek + config tests pass.
